docs/plans/plan-2026-05-11-artifact-visibility.md:12:The website autogen plan (`plan-2026-05-11-website-autogen.md`) needs a per-artifact signal for "should this publish to the public website?" The first-draft autogen plan proposed inventing a website-specific `ddx.public: true|false` frontmatter field. Both the self-review and an opus second-opinion converged: visibility is a property **of the artifact**, not of any one renderer. It belongs in FEAT-005 (the artifact convention spec), where other consumers — search, doctor diagnostics, doc-graph filters, future renderers — can also reach it.
docs/plans/plan-2026-05-11-artifact-visibility.md:35:| `draft` | Work in progress. Same exclusion as `internal` for public renderers, but may surface in `ddx doctor` / project-internal "draft" sections. |
docs/plans/plan-2026-05-11-artifact-visibility.md:54:- **`ddx doctor`** can optionally surface counts ("12 internal FEATs, 3 public, 4 drafts") for awareness; not required.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/doctor.go:418 `reportSkillInstallTopology(projectRoot)` computes a global path and a project path with statuses (doctor.go:436/441/444 `lazy-resolves-to-global`) and prints two lines at doctor.go:447-448: `Global Install (%s) — %s` and `Project Install (%s) — %s`. These lines lack a regression test.
- The dual-topology decision and precedence are not yet fully reflected in the spec/docs: ADR-027 (skill-install-topology), library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md "How this skill works", and CLAUDE.md "Persona System".

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a test asserting `ddx doctor` output contains both the `Global Install (` and `Project Install (` lines with a correct status (`ok` | `missing` | `lazy-resolves-to-global`) given seeded/absent tiers.
- Update docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-skill-install-topology.md to document install-to-agent (global) vs install-in-project (in-tree + convention) and the project→global→baked-in precedence.
- Update CLAUDE.md "Persona System" section to state the install precedence, and library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md "How this skill works" to document install locations. Land all spec/doc edits in this bead's commit.

NON-SCOPE
- Install/init/update behavioral changes — sibling beads (this bead consumes their behavior for the doctor report only).
- Removing the per-project install (both modes coexist).
- Migrating legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution).
- Sibling install/update beads (doctor reports the layers they create); land this bead after they merge so the report reflects real behavior.","events_attachment":"ddx-03421468/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-03421468","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:cli","area:plugin","area:docs","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-b76dd9d6","status":"closed","title":"doctor + docs: report both install layers and land skill-install-topology spec updates","updated_at":"2026-06-03T09:03:55.755036689Z","work-no-changes-count":1}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:9:{"acceptance":"1. `ddxroot.GlobalDir()` returns `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global`, honoring an overridden XDG_DATA_HOME, verified by TestGlobalDir_HonorsXDGDataHome.
2. `registry.ResolvePlugin` returns layer "project" with the project path when both project and global copies exist — TestPluginLookup_PrefersProjectOverGlobal asserts the project copy wins.
3. `registry.ResolvePlugin` returns layer "global" with the global path when the project copy is absent but the global copy exists — TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal.
4. `registry.ResolvePlugin` returns layer "baked-in" for the default `ddx` plugin when neither project nor global copy exists, and a non-nil error for any other unresolved name — TestPluginLookup_BakedInDefaultOnly.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/ddxroot/... ./internal/registry/... green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"f4f79067f34de800edcb3151a2ed74b51407bb2b","created_at":"2026-06-02T01:50:49.073172731Z","description":"PROBLEM
DDx has no runtime concept of a "global" plugin install nor a precedence order for resolving a plugin name. Every install/lookup path is hardcoded to the per-project tree via ddxroot.Path(). Before `--global` install (sibling bead) or `ddx init --global` (sibling bead) can exist, there must be a single resolver that, given a plugin name, returns its on-disk path by checking the project layer first, then a global layer, then the baked-in default.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/registry/installer.go:66 `InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, src, projectRoot)` only knows the project root; there is no global target.
- cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:75 `Path()` resolves only the project root (in-tree `<project>/.ddx` or convention `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/...` at line 86). There is no `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global` helper.
- cli/internal/skills/install.go:28 `Install(src, projectRoot, opts)` and all callers assume a single project destination.
- No `ResolvePlugin`-style function exists anywhere under cli/internal/registry.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add `GlobalDir()` to cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go returning `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global` (reuse the existing `xdgDataHome()` helper at ddxroot.go:89). The plugins root is `GlobalDir()/plugins`.
2. Add `registry.ResolvePlugin(ctx, projectRoot, name string) (path string, layer string, error)` implementing precedence:
   - `ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)/plugins/<name>` if the dir exists → layer "project".
   - `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>` if it exists → layer "global".
   - baked-in default (defaultplugin.FS, the `ddx` plugin only) → layer "baked-in"; return an error for any other unresolved name.
3. Export the layer string so doctor/init/install (sibling beads) can report which layer answered.

NON-SCOPE
- Wiring `--global` into install/update commands (sibling bead: install/update --global).
- `ddx init --global` and agent-tier symlinks (sibling bead: init topology).
- `ddx doctor` reporting (sibling bead: doctor + specs).
- Removing the existing per-project install. Both layers coexist.

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — `ddxroot.Path()` and the new `GlobalDir()` must share the same XDG_DATA_HOME convention agreed there. Sibling beads in this set depend on this resolver.","events_attachment":"ddx-04566a8f/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-04566a8f","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:config","area:plugin","area:cli","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-20de9a81","status":"closed","title":"registry: add two-layer plugin resolution (project → global → baked-in) with global XDG path helper","updated_at":"2026-06-02T01:56:37.269486624Z"}

2. `ddx install <name>` (no --global, in-tree mode) installs to `<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/` and creates project-tier links under `<project>/.claude/skills/<name>` and `<project>/.agents/skills/<name>` — TestInstallProject_InTreeLinks.
3. `ddx install <name>` (no --global, convention mode) installs to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/` and creates project-tier links pointing into that XDG plugins path — TestInstallProject_ConventionLinks.

7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"92b4821c473537ebe844ccbd407e656a2aded9b5","created_at":"2026-06-02T01:50:49.131007555Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-06f820f4","depends_on_id":"ddx-72868e5e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-06-02T21:01:28Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-06f820f4","depends_on_id":"ddx-ae23101f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-06-03T05:34:14Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
`ddx install` and `ddx update` only ever target the per-project plugin tree. There is no way to install a skill/persona/plugin once per machine, so operators must repeat installs in every project. Three install destinations must be supported: global (XDG), project in-tree (`<project>/.ddx`), and project convention (XDG projects/...).

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go:36 `RunE: f.runInstall` and its flag set (install.go:38-39) have no `--global` flag; install always targets the project under `.ddx/plugins/<name>/` (install.go:27-28).
- cli/cmd/update.go:57 `runUpdate` and update.go:194 `performUpdate` operate only on the working dir's project tree; update.go:112 `enumerateSkillUpdateTargets(workingDir)` is project-scoped.
- Both route through ddxroot.Path() unconditionally and never reference the global layer.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add `--global` bool flag to `ddx install` (cli/cmd/install.go) and `ddx update` (cli/cmd/update.go).
2. install --global: write content to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/` (via `ddxroot.GlobalDir()` from the resolver bead) and create symlinks/copies into agent-tier paths `~/.claude/skills/<name>` and `~/.agents/skills/<name>` (copy on platforms lacking symlink support).
3. install (no --global): keep writing to `ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/` and link into `<project>/.claude/skills/<name>` AND `<project>/.agents/skills/<name>`. In in-tree mode the target is `<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>`; in convention mode it is `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>` and the project agent-tier links point into that XDG path.
4. update --global updates only the global plugin tree; update (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode (in-tree vs convention) is active, via `enumerateSkillUpdateTargets`.

NON-SCOPE
- The plugin resolver/precedence (sibling bead: registry two-layer resolution) — consume `ddxroot.GlobalDir()` from it.
- `ddx init --global` first-time setup (sibling bead: init topology).
- `ddx doctor` reporting and spec/doc updates (sibling bead: doctor + specs).
- Removing per-project install; both modes coexist.

DEPS

5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"5ed2db7ea6ad073f55cde6f3110336b3dddb8194","created_at":"2026-06-02T03:19:04.419539201Z","description":"PROBLEM
First-time per-machine setup has no entry point. The intended model is that `ddx init --global` performs one-time machine setup by installing the default `ddx` plugin into the global layer, creating agent-tier skill links, and writing global convention defaults.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/init.go:73 `runInit` has no `--global` mode branch.
- cli/cmd/init.go:271-284 only performs eager project-local default plugin installation via `registry.InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, defaultplugin.FS(), workingDir)`; there is no path that installs the default plugin to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx`.
- There is no writer for `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml`.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add a `--global` bool flag to `ddx init` in `cli/cmd/init.go`.
2. When `--global` is set, install the bundled default `ddx` plugin to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx` using the install-global path from the sibling install/update --global bead.
3. Create agent-tier links `~/.claude/skills/ddx` and `~/.agents/skills/ddx` pointing into `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/`, reusing the helper from the sibling install/update --global bead.
4. Write `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml` with convention defaults.

NON-SCOPE
- The resolver precedence itself (sibling bead: registry two-layer resolution).
- install/update `--global` wiring (sibling bead).
- `ddx doctor` reporting and spec/doc updates (sibling bead: doctor + specs).
- Migrating any `~/.config/ddx/` legacy paths.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution).
- sibling install/update --global bead (reuse its install-global path and agent-tier link helper).

PARENT

3. `cd cli && lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"880cfacf58d5c1c5135ed58327dad027280bc761","created_at":"2026-06-06T03:14:51.058731186Z","description":"PROBLEM
The worker still treats `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale` as a recoverable no-progress outcome even when the just-created children still point back to the attempted bead. That leaves the same bead getting reclaimed forever while the children are structurally blocked.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` already has no-progress and candidate-skip machinery, but it does not check for the specific back-edge case where a fresh child carries the attempted bead in its deps. Without that guard, the worker continues to re-claim the parent instead of escalating.

PROPOSED FIX
When a run ends with `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale` and any freshly-created child still depends on the attempted bead, emit a durable `operator_attention` event with reason `parent_back_edge`, park the bead, and stop re-claiming it. Add a regression named `TestWorkHaltsOnParentBackEdge` that proves the worker exits the loop rather than retrying the parent.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change the dependency validation commands, doctor repair logic, prompt templates, or lifecycle docs in this bead.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_decompose_test.go (if the test fixture needs child deps)

Out-of-scope:
- cli/internal/bead/store.go
- cli/internal/bead/doctor.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go
- cli/internal/agent/lifecycle_dispatch.go","events_attachment":"ddx-0836dde5/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-0836dde5","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:tracker","area:lifecycle","area:decomposition","kind:safety","kind:correctness","ac-quality:needs-refinement"],"parent":"ddx-e19185aa","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-1089ef73","status":"closed","title":"Halt work on parent_back_edge no-progress loops","updated_at":"2026-06-06T23:20:29.088435667Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:25:{"acceptance":"1. `cd cli && go test ./cmd -run 'TestWork_BlocksStaleSourceBinaryBeforeQueueScan|TestTry_BlocksStaleSourceBinaryBeforeClaim|TestVersionWarnsStaleSourceBinary|TestCheckVersionGate' -count=1` passes.
2. `TestWork_BlocksStaleSourceBinaryBeforeQueueScan` proves no bead is claimed and no readiness/run service call occurs when the DDx source tree is ahead of the binary commit.
3. `TestTry_BlocksStaleSourceBinaryBeforeClaim` proves `ddx try <id>` fails before claim/attempt evidence when the same stale-source condition is present.
4. `ddx work` and `ddx try` error text includes the project root, binary commit, source HEAD, and `make install` recovery instruction.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","claimed-at":"2026-05-15T08:33:11Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1283521","closing_commit_sha":"55c22e74e2e4b9f5a012b806b153a9bb10144f17","created_at":"2026-05-15T03:40:47.108776748Z","description":"PROBLEM
Operators can run `ddx work` from the DDx source checkout with an installed binary built from an older commit. That produced live workers still using old readiness/routing behavior even after the local source tree had been fixed. `ddx version` can warn, but `ddx work` and `ddx try` still proceed to claim beads and perform actual builds with stale code.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/cmd/command_factory.go:603` implements `warnIfInstalledBinaryBehindSource`, but it is only called from the `version` command path at `cli/cmd/command_factory.go:511`.
- `cli/cmd/command_factory.go:169` runs the global pre-run version gate, but it checks `.ddx/versions.yaml` semver only and does not block a stale installed binary inside the DDx source repository.
- `cli/cmd/work.go:130` and `cli/cmd/try.go:116` resolve the project root for execution, but neither refuses to build from an older DDx binary when the project root is the DDx source repo.

PROPOSED FIX
Add an execution preflight for `ddx work` and `ddx try`: when running inside the DDx source repository, and the binary reports a concrete build commit that is an ancestor of the source checkout `HEAD` with one or more commits ahead, fail before queue scan, claim, readiness, or dispatch. The error must name the binary commit, source `HEAD`, project root, and the exact recovery command (`make install` from the source checkout). Keep `ddx version`, `ddx doctor`, `ddx init`, `ddx upgrade`, `help`, and `completion` usable for recovery. Do not make this a warning for worker commands.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/cmd/command_factory.go`
- `cli/cmd/work.go`
- `cli/cmd/try.go`
- `cli/cmd/version_gate_test.go`
- `cli/cmd/work_test.go`
- `cli/cmd/try_test.go`

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change release semver gating against `.ddx/versions.yaml`.
- Do not auto-run `make install` or mutate the operator's PATH.
- Do not block dev binaries with `Version=dev` or binaries without a known commit.

Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- The orchestrator's 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning, when emitted alongside the parent-back-edge state, is upgraded from WARN to ERROR with the offending bead ID logged. Test: dispatch_logs_error_on_root_mutation_with_back_edge.
- Lifecycle schema documents that 'parent' and 'deps' MUST be disjoint and that 'deps' MUST be acyclic with the parent-tree. docs/ddx/lifecycle-rules.md (or equivalent) updated; ADR file if no governing artifact already exists.
- Existing valid graphs continue to pass 'ddx bead doctor' without --fix and without regression — regression test against a pre-existing tracker snapshot.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.","events_attachment":"ddx-0c96c8b1/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-0c96c8b1","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:tracker","area:lifecycle","area:decomposition","kind:safety","kind:correctness","ac-quality:needs-refinement","decomposed"],"parent":"ddx-e19185aa","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-80d6ce75","status":"closed","title":"Reject bead dep edges that re-introduce the parent or any ancestor: part 3","updated_at":"2026-06-07T05:37:28.262438601Z","work-no-changes-count":2}

Convention mode (per ddx-ad338ec3) bootstraps an XDG project dir but has no source for skills, personas, or the default ddx plugin. Either every project re-downloads the plugin on first use, or the user must have a global install. The latter is the better answer: install once per machine, all convention-mode projects share.

ROOT CAUSE
There is no per-user install path today. `ddx init` only writes per-project state. Skills/personas live in the project's .ddx/plugins/ (in-tree) or are missing entirely (convention).

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add `ddx init --global` flag (and corresponding code path in cli/cmd/init.go). When set:
   - Resolve target path: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ (defaults to ~/.local/share/ddx/global/).
   - Install the default ddx plugin (library/ contents) into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/.
   - Write a default ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults (library URL, branch, etc.).
   - Idempotent: re-running upgrades to a newer plugin version but does not destroy operator state.

2. Convention mode reads ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ as the second precedence tier (in-tree > global > hardcoded). If --global has never been run, fall back to baked-in hardcoded defaults — convention mode still works without it; --global is an optimization for users who want offline-cacheable plugin content.

3. Document the install path in ddx doctor: a new check "Global Install" reports whether ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ exists and points operators to `ddx init --global` if it does not.

4. Do NOT remove the existing per-project plugin install (in-tree mode at .ddx/plugins/<name>/). Both coexist.

NON-SCOPE
- In-project ddx init manifest write (E4 bead).
- Migration from legacy ~/.config/ddx/ or ~/.ddx/ if such exist on disk (none expected; today there is no global install).
- Plugin update / upgrade flow for the global install (use existing `ddx update` mechanism; verify it works against the new path).

DEPS
ddx-564ea1cf (E1) — resolver must exist.

Plugins resolve only to project-local paths today. internal/registry has no concept of a machine-global plugin tree (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>) nor a precedence order for runtime lookup. This child lands the path resolver and precedence that the --global install/update/init/doctor children all build on.

ROOT CAUSE
- No resolver maps (projectRoot, name) -> a concrete plugin dir with precedence.
- cli/internal/registry/registry.go exposes BuiltinRegistry/Find only; there is no GlobalPluginsDir / ResolvePluginDir.
- The XDG data-home helper xdgDataHome() in cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:89 is unexported; project root comes from ddxroot.Path() (ddxroot.go:75).
- cli/internal/registry/installer.go rejects any home-rooted (~) install target at installer.go:43, :79, :135 (FEAT-015). That guard must stay for project-scope installs; this child does NOT write any global tree, only resolves paths.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add registry.GlobalPluginsDir() string returning filepath.Join(<xdg-data-home>, 'ddx', 'global', 'plugins'), honoring XDG_DATA_HOME and the ~/.local/share fallback (mirror ddxroot.xdgDataHome semantics; export a shared helper rather than duplicating).
2. Add registry.ResolvePluginDir(ctx context.Context, projectRoot, name string) (dir, scope string, ok bool) with precedence:
   a. ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)/plugins/<name> if it exists -> scope 'project'.
   b. GlobalPluginsDir()/<name> if it exists -> scope 'global'.
   c. baked-in default plugin (only name=='ddx', via internal/registry/defaultplugin) -> scope 'baked-in'.
   d. otherwise ok=false.
3. Leave FEAT-015 ~-target rejection in installer.go untouched.

NON-SCOPE: wiring --global into install/update/init/doctor (sibling children); writing/symlinking the global tree.

DEPS: none new (parent dep ddx-06cbaa90 closed; ddxroot.Path already lands).","id":"ddx-18e55cd2","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:config"," area:plugin"," area:cli"," kind:feature"," phase:3"," initiative:zero-footprint"," principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"status":"open","title":"registry: global plugins dir resolution + project→global→baked-in plugin lookup precedence","updated_at":"2026-06-02T01:47:34.480115811Z"}

The decomposition path that fans a too-large bead into executable children still wires the parent into the child dependency set. That recreates the exact back-edge this parent bead is about.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4675-4701` currently creates each child with `Parent: parent.ID` and then explicitly adds dependency edges from the parent to every child. The static prompt text in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2490-2577` and the guardrail snapshots in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_guardrails_test.go` still teach the decomposition flow to use the parent as a dependency edge, so the bug survives both the runtime path and the emitted instructions.

PROPOSED FIX
Change the programmatic decomposition path and the tracked prompt/guardrail text so generated children never add the parent to their deps. Keep only legitimate child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges. Add a golden e2e test named `decompose_does_not_self_dep` that creates a parent with N children and asserts every child's deps set excludes the parent.

NON-SCOPE
Do not touch bead store validation, doctor repair, work-loop back-edge remediation, or lifecycle docs in this bead.

- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_guardrails_test.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_decompose_test.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go (if needed for the golden e2e assertion)

Out-of-scope:
- cli/internal/bead/store.go
- cli/cmd/bead.go
- cli/internal/bead/doctor.go
- cli/internal/agent/lifecycle_dispatch.go","events_attachment":"ddx-1bcb9827/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-1bcb9827","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:tracker","area:lifecycle","area:decomposition","kind:safety","kind:correctness","ac-quality:needs-refinement"],"parent":"ddx-e19185aa","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-1f35afe4","status":"closed","title":"Remove parent dependency edges from decomposition","updated_at":"2026-06-06T20:29:24.518264435Z"}

`ddx work --watch` still stops the entire drain when the project checkout contains implementation-file dirt before the next bead dispatch. During the Fizeau monitor on 2026-05-15, `fizeau-126f03c0` landed successfully, then the watcher attempted `fizeau-f02ada30` and exited with operator attention: project worktree `/Users/erik/Projects/fizeau` had uncommitted implementation changes in internal/routing/engine.go, internal/routing/score.go, service.go, service_aliveness.go, service_execute.go, service_execute_dispatch.go, and service_routing.go. The operator had to stash/restore files by hand before the queue could continue.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:575-599` treats any non-DDx dirty implementation path as a hard pre-dispatch checkpoint refusal. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1880-1912` converts that refusal into `operator_attention`, unclaims the candidate, logs “commit or clean”, and returns from the work loop. That behavior is safe for arbitrary user WIP, but it is not robust for DDx-managed watch drains where leaked lifecycle/root edits or generated artifacts must be preserved and the worker should keep draining.

PROPOSED FIX
Replace the hard stop with an audited preserve-and-clean path for pre-dispatch dirt in managed `ddx work --watch` flows: write a named preservation artifact or git stash/ref containing the dirty patch, record the dirty paths and preserve handle in the attempt/work log, restore the affected paths to HEAD, unclaim the candidate without losing the patch, and keep the watch loop alive so the next scan can proceed. The implementation must still avoid silently discarding operator WIP: the preserve handle and affected paths must be visible in events/log output and the root must be clean before dispatching the next bead.

NON-SCOPE
Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`. Do not weaken checkpoint filtering for `.ddx` bookkeeping paths. Do not squash/rewrite execute-bead history. Do not hide dirty root evidence without a recoverable preservation handle.","events_attachment":"ddx-1c952784/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-1c952784","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:work","kind:bug","operator-ux","release-blocker"],"priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-1cbf0995","status":"closed","title":"work: preserve and clear pre-dispatch implementation dirt instead of stopping watch","updated_at":"2026-05-15T17:15:23.967217911Z"}

Operators who want one Claude Code / Codex install per machine need a `--global` surface so a plugin is written once to the machine-wide tree and linked into the home-level harness skill directories, available across every project without per-project setup.

ROOT CAUSE
- `runInstall` (cli/cmd/install.go:54) reads the `--global` flag (install.go:49,57) and routes target resolution through `resolveInstallDirs(global)` (install.go:184). When global is true it sets `installRoot = ddxroot.GlobalDir()` and skill dirs to `~/.agents/skills` and `~/.claude/skills` (install.go:185-192); state file selection is via `selectInstallState(global)` (install.go:202-215). Local installs route the same way in `installLocal` (install.go:358, global branch install.go:450-507).
- Update: `performGlobalUpdate` (cli/cmd/update.go:300) operates exclusively on `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/` via `registry.InstallPackage(adjustedPkg, ddxroot.GlobalDir())` (update.go:337) and saves the global state (update.go:346); the `--global` flag is parsed in `extractUpdateOptions` (update.go:395) into `UpdateOptions.Global` (update.go:35) and dispatched from `runUpdate` (update.go:65).

PROPOSED FIX
Ensure `ddx install <name> --global` writes the plugin to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/` and creates symlink-or-copy entries under `~/.claude/skills/<name>` and `~/.agents/skills/<name>`, and does NOT touch the project's `.claude/skills` / `.agents/skills`. Ensure `ddx update --global` updates only the global plugin tree and global state, never the project tree. Expose `--global` consistently on the plugin query surface (`ddx plugin list` / `installed`).

NON-SCOPE
- The no-flag project install in either mode (slice 3).
- `ddx init --global` first-time setup and doctor reporting (slice 4).
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:79:{"acceptance":"1. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestIsTransientGitContention|TestFinalizeDurableAudit.*TrackerLock' -count=1` passes.
2. A unit test asserts `isTransientGitContention` returns true for an error wrapping `TrackerLockTimeoutError`.
3. A unit test asserts `isTransientGitContention` returns true for the literal substring "tracker lock timeout" as a defense-in-depth fallback.
4. A unit test of `finalizeDurableAuditOrStop` injects a tracker-lock-timeout error and asserts: (a) `result.OperatorAttention` is NOT set, (b) the durable_audit_transient event is emitted, (c) the function returns false (don't stop).
5. `cd cli && go test ./... -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"f5ad95d750e192a6579517c58ab83d69c1216ec0","created_at":"2026-05-29T02:21:39.52547268Z","description":"PROBLEM: When two or more `ddx work` workers run concurrently against the
same project and worker B reaches `FinalizeDurableAudit` at the same moment
that worker A holds the tracker lock, worker B times out, and the loop
classifies the timeout as `operator_attention` and exits — even though
nothing requires operator action. The other worker just needs to finish.

OBSERVED 2026-05-28 in niflheim drain with two concurrent workers:
- Worker1 (pid 424293) was committing tracker state for niflheim-996afcc8.
- Worker2 (pid 681801), simultaneously finalizing niflheim-e43f04bb (an
  ADR-051 cold-tier PUT concurrency implementation that had already run to
  completion — 66091 tokens, 32m8.5s), hit `tracker lock timeout (max
  elapsed, lock: /home/erik/Projects/niflheim/.ddx/.git-tracker.lock,
  owner pid: 424293)` while trying to commit durable audit outputs.
- Worker2 exited with `operator attention required`, releasing
  niflheim-e43f04bb back to `open` without committing the audit trail,
  and leaving these paths dirty: `.ddx/beads.jsonl`,
  `.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl`, `.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl`,
  `.ddx/attachments`.

The work was successful — 32 minutes of agent time produced a clean
implementation and commit. The lock race nuked the audit attribution and
forced the worker to stop. Re-running it would either repeat the agent run
(burning more tokens) or hit `already_satisfied` (with the worktree-leak
documented in `ddx-274943fc`).

ROOT CAUSE: `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1262-1309`
(`finalizeDurableAuditOrStop`) calls `runtime.FinalizeDurableAudit` and
branches on `isTransientGitContention(err.Error())` to decide retry vs
operator-attention. The matcher at
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1019-1034` covers `.git/index.lock`
(via `gitlock.IsIndexLockError`) and the strings "unable to write new index
file", "cannot lock ref", "unable to update the ref" — but **does not cover
the DDx-specific tracker-lock error format** emitted by
`cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:243`:

  "tracker lock timeout (%s, lock: %s, owner pid: %s)"

The tracker lock is a DDx-internal ordering lock around tracker writes
(`.ddx/.git-tracker.lock`), held briefly during tracker-state mutations.
Concurrent worker contention on this lock is structurally identical to
the `.git/index.lock` case the existing matcher handles, and the comment
on `isTransientGitContention` explicitly states the design intent:

  "A persistent cause (e.g. ENOSPC) simply keeps releasing+retrying the
   bead, which is still preferable to halting the whole unattended drain."

PROPOSED FIX: Extend `isTransientGitContention` to recognise the DDx
tracker-lock-timeout string class. Recommended implementation:

  1. Export a stable marker constant (or recogniser) from
     `cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go` rather than string-matching
     across packages. Suggested: a sentinel error type
     `TrackerLockTimeoutError` wrapping the existing fields (lockDir,
     ownerPID), with an `Is(err)` helper.
  2. Update `FinalizeDurableAudit` and its callers to wrap the timeout
     error with this sentinel, so callers can use `errors.Is` instead of
     substring matching.
  3. In `isTransientGitContention` at `execute_bead_loop.go:1019`, add a
     branch that returns true for `errors.Is(err, TrackerLockTimeoutErr)`.
     Keep the existing substring fallbacks for defense in depth.
  4. Confirm via test that worker2 in the observed scenario survives the
     lock timeout, re-enters the loop, and successfully commits on a
     subsequent iteration when the lock releases.

NON-SCOPE:
- Changing the tracker-lock retry policy itself (timeout duration, backoff
  curve) — only changing how the loop classifies the lock-timeout outcome.
- Changing concurrent-worker safety guarantees on tracker writes.
- Adding queue-level fairness or worker coordination (out of scope for
  this bead).
- Fixing the orphan-worktree leak that compounds this issue — tracked
  separately in `ddx-274943fc`.

IN-SCOPE FILES:
- cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go (export sentinel error type)
- cli/internal/agent/durable_audit.go (wrap timeout with sentinel where
  it bubbles up — verify by reading line 41 and 127)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (extend `isTransientGitContention`)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go (new test for tracker-lock
  classification)

OUT-OF-SCOPE FILES:
- gitlock package (already correct for .git/index.lock; do not modify)
- cli/cmd/work.go (exit-reason rendering unchanged)
- Any other isTransientGitContention call sites — only the durable-audit
  branch matters for this bug.","events_attachment":"ddx-2189a07f/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-2189a07f","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:execution","area:bead","kind:bug","kind:reliability","phase:iterate","spec:FEAT-010"],"needs-human-reason":"consecutive_wedge","needs-human-since":"2026-05-31T06:52:49Z","needs-human-source":"ddx work","needs-human-suggested-action":"investigate the repeated wedge (resolver hang / phase-empty heartbeats) and clear the operator-attention state once resolved","needs-human-summary":"bead wedged on 2 consecutive claims (>= threshold 2; last wedge "progress_watchdog"); stopped re-claiming and flagged for operator attention","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-eb508f6b","spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: treat 'tracker lock timeout' as transient git contention (currently misclassifies concurrent-worker lock race as operator_attention and exits)","updated_at":"2026-06-06T02:19:02.318974175Z"}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:87:{"acceptance":"1. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestExecuteBead.*Cleanup|TestCleanupAttemptWorktree' -count=1` passes.
2. A unit test exists for each of these non-success outcomes asserting WorktreeRemove was called: provider_connectivity, no_evidence_produced, execution_failed, structural_validation_failed, land_conflict, post_run_check_failed.
3. A unit test asserts the success-path classifier does NOT call WorktreeRemove from the new hook (landing path retains ownership).
4. A unit test asserts `ddx try --no-merge` preserves the worktree regardless of outcome.
5. `cd cli && go test ./... -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"e979bcd786ff98bd4767596dd1ed2dbe76cddb0c","created_at":"2026-05-29T02:02:14.061932044Z","description":"PROBLEM: When a `ddx try`/`ddx work` attempt fails with any non-success
outcome (`provider_connectivity`, `no_evidence_produced`, `execution_failed`,
`structural_validation_failed`, `land_conflict`, `post_run_check_failed`),
its execute-bead worktree under `~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-<bead>-<attemptID>/`
is not removed. Cleanup happens only when a *subsequent attempt of the same bead*
is launched and `RecoverOrphans` runs as pre-spawn sweep.

OBSERVED 2026-05-28 in niflheim drain: a 6.6 GB worktree
`.execute-bead-wt-niflheim-07f6f8ff-20260528T164209-f179349f` remained 9+
hours after its attempt failed with `provider_connectivity` (bragi 502).
The bead's retry resolved as `already_satisfied` without spawning a new
execute-bead worktree (the AC was already met by partially-landed commits
from the failed attempt), so `RecoverOrphans` was never called for that
bead — the leak is permanent until another attempt at the same bead runs.

The user reports seeing this pattern consistently across drains, not just
on the one observed bead.

ROOT CAUSE: `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:393-411`
defines `RecoverOrphans` with the docstring "the parent's responsibility —
call it before spawning new workers so stale worktrees from crashed previous
attempts do not accumulate." The function matches strictly by
`basenamePrefix := ExecuteBeadWtPrefix + beadID + "-"` and is only invoked
on a new-attempt pre-spawn path. Two leak conditions follow:

  (a) Bead's retry succeeds via `already_satisfied` (no new worktree
      spawned because the AC check resolves before agent invocation).
      The orphan from the prior failed attempt is never matched.
  (b) Bead is closed for any reason after a failed attempt and is never
      re-attempted (most common in real flows — failures often resolve
      via decomposition into different child beads, never returning to
      the original).

The post-attempt outcome classifier at
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1966` (Classify worker outcome:
task_succeeded / task_failed / task_no_changes / ...) is the natural place
to call `WorktreeRemove` for non-success classes, but does not.
The existing landing path at
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1088,1329,1679` calls
`gitOps.RemoveWorktree` correctly for landing-time temp worktrees, but
those are different (per-landing) worktrees, not the per-attempt execute-bead
worktree under `~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/`.

PROPOSED FIX: Add an attempt-finalize cleanup hook that runs after outcome
classification (around `execute_bead.go:1966-2017`) and removes the
per-attempt execute-bead worktree when the outcome is anything other than
`task_succeeded`. Preserve only when:
  1. The operator explicitly opted in via `ddx try --no-merge` (preserve
     for inspection regardless of outcome).
  2. An `executions_mirror.go` write or evidence-capture step requires the
     worktree to persist past classification (verify by reading the call
     chain at `execute_bead.go:2069-2114`).

Implementation steps the executing agent should follow:
  1. Read `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1423-2017` to confirm the
     outcome-classification flow and identify the right post-classify hook
     point.
  2. Add a helper `cleanupAttemptWorktree(gitOps, workDir, wtPath, outcome,
     preserveFlag)` that calls `gitOps.WorktreeRemove(workDir, wtPath)` for
     non-success outcomes unless `preserveFlag` is set.
  3. Wire it into the outcome-classification path.
  4. Add a unit test asserting the cleanup branch for each non-success
     outcome class, and a negative test confirming success-path keeps the
     worktree (landing path owns that cleanup).
  5. Update `RecoverOrphans` docstring to reflect that it's now a
     belt-and-suspenders sweep, not the primary cleanup path.

NON-SCOPE:
- Changing `RecoverOrphans` behavior or call sites (keep it; it covers
  hard-crash cases the new hook can't reach).
- Adding a periodic background sweeper or `ddx doctor` flag for stale
  worktrees (potential follow-up).
- Changing the worktree path under `~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/`.
- Changing outcome-classification logic or outcome taxonomy.
- Adding telemetry/metrics for leak rates.

IN-SCOPE FILES:
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (add the cleanup hook + helper)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_test.go or sibling _test file (add
  cleanup tests for each non-success outcome)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go (docstring update on
  RecoverOrphans only; behavior unchanged)

OUT-OF-SCOPE FILES:
- execute_bead_land.go — landing-time temp-worktree cleanup is correct
  and untouched.
- Any code under cli/cmd/ — no CLI surface change required.
- docs/ — documentation update is downstream of the spec-evolution bead
  ddx-828a1b8f (worktree resource isolation) and not required here.","events_attachment":"ddx-274943fc/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-274943fc","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:execution","kind:bug","kind:operability","phase:iterate","spec:FEAT-010"],"needs-human-reason":"consecutive_wedge","needs-human-since":"2026-05-31T05:20:40Z","needs-human-source":"ddx work","needs-human-suggested-action":"investigate the repeated wedge (resolver hang / phase-empty heartbeats) and clear the operator-attention state once resolved","needs-human-summary":"bead wedged on 2 consecutive claims (>= threshold 2; last wedge "progress_watchdog"); stopped re-claiming and flagged for operator attention","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-90ab9f5e","spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: clean up worktree on non-success attempt outcomes (currently leaks on failed-then-retried-as-already_satisfied path)","updated_at":"2026-06-06T02:28:44.767198655Z"}

Without `--global`, install and update must target the project's ddx root, which differs by mode: in-tree (`<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>`) versus convention (`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>`). Either way the project-tier agent skill links (`<project>/.claude/skills` and `<project>/.agents/skills`) must point into whichever plugins tree is active.

ROOT CAUSE
- `resolveInstallDirs(global=false)` (cli/cmd/install.go:184, project branch install.go:195-197) sets `installRoot = ddxroot.Path(ctx, f.WorkingDir)` and skill dirs to `<workingDir>/.agents/skills` and `<workingDir>/.claude/skills`. `ddxroot.Path` (cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:75) returns the in-tree `.ddx` path when `<project>/.ddx` exists and the convention `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/...` path otherwise, so mode selection is implicit.
- `installLocal` non-global branch derives `ddxRootDir := ddxroot.Path(...)` (install.go:453) for the same routing.
- Update: the non-global path `performUpdate` (cli/cmd/update.go:204) uses `ddxroot.JoinProject(workingDir, ...)` (e.g. update.go:227) so it updates the project install in whichever mode is active; mode is not an explicit flag but follows `ddxroot`.

PROPOSED FIX
Ensure `ddx install <name>` (no `--global`) in in-tree mode writes to `<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/` and links `<project>/.claude/skills/<name>` and `<project>/.agents/skills/<name>`; in convention mode (no `<project>/.ddx/`) writes to the XDG project plugins path and links the project-tier skill dirs into that path. Ensure `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active.

NON-SCOPE
- The `--global` install/update surface (slice 2).
- `ddx init` behavior and doctor reporting (slice 4).
- Removing either mode — in-tree and convention coexist.

DEPS
- Slice 1 (plugin lookup precedence) and ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — project paths route through `ddxroot.Path()`.","events_attachment":"ddx-2b9c26d7/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-2b9c26d7","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:plugin","area:cli","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas","parent:ddx-602ae31a","dep:ddx-06cbaa90"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-c7bae1d6","status":"closed","title":"project install surface: ddx install (no flag) and ddx update honor in-tree vs convention mode","updated_at":"2026-06-03T12:16:40.973821743Z","work-no-changes-count":1}

A live Niflheim worker audit on 2026-06-06 showed that `ddx work` is not yet a stable release-grade autonomous worker. Strong-model escalation exists, but it is not reliably tied to the class of failure. The worker spent repeated attempts on structural or system failures that stronger models cannot fix, while automatic review evidence was empty on attempts that were recorded as successful. This violates the DDx engineering principle "Cheapest Viable, Escalate on Evidence" (docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:39-45): review, runtime, or retry evidence should determine escalation, and only lower-power inability to meet acceptance criteria should push to stronger routes. It also threatens FEAT-010 task-execution reliability because beads can be retried, escalated, or closed without a typed, auditable reason.

This bead is a Niflheim incident/regression closure bead. It is not a duplicate of the narrower queue items listed under INTERSECTIONS; instead it captures the external evidence DDx workers will not otherwise have, and requires a DDx-side regression suite proving those observed scenarios classify correctly before rerun, escalation, operator attention, or close.

BACKGROUND AND OBSERVED NIFLHEIM EVIDENCE
The evidence was gathered from Niflheim worker logs and result bundles, but those files are not load-bearing for this bead. The relevant facts are copied here because an isolated DDx worker cannot access the Niflheim repository or `/tmp` logs.

1. Decomposed parent kept consuming attempts and escalated to a stronger model anyway.
- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-64217cc5`, title "Instrument cold replicator with structured per-operation S3 tracing".
- Observed result bundle: `.ddx/executions/20260606T152006-a4ca7254/result.json` plus worker logs from 2026-06-05/2026-06-06.
- Routing showed escalation exists: repeated routes started at `gpt-5.4-mini` with requested min powers 5, 7, 8, and 9, then reached `gpt-5.5` at requested min power 10.
- Metrics on the bead showed `attempt_count=24`, `total_tokens=19371353`, and average duration about `244112ms`.
- Despite triage recommending `close_decomposed_or_mark_execution_ineligible`, retries continued until the latest event recorded:
  `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale`
  `powerClass=standard`
  `rationale: status: open`
  `orchestrator_action: decompose`
  `reason: parent bead exceeds the Step 0 size threshold and is already decomposed into focused child beads for tracing, counters, tests, and runbook work.`
  `child_beads: niflheim-f03c61a2, niflheim-df407aa8, niflheim-7a421ede, niflheim-0bbed6c9`
  `outcome_reason=decomposed`
- Expected DDx behavior: once the worker has reliable evidence that the bead is a decomposed parent or execution-ineligible parent, no further implementation attempt or model escalation should occur. It should mark execution-ineligible/proposed/operator-attention according to policy and move on.

2. Land/evidence coordination failures were treated like implementation failure and retried expensively.
- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-c75aba30`, title "Materializer per-merge memory footprint OOMs even at concurrent_limit=8".
- Two expensive attempts both used `gpt-5.4-mini`; no useful stronger-model escalation was observed.
- Metrics showed `attempt_count=2`, `total_tokens=65513510`, and average duration about `3306461.5ms`.
- First attempt result: `.ddx/executions/20260606T113550-7b29ee42/result.json`.
  - `status=execution_failed`, `outcome=error`.
  - Implementation result commit was `7aaeb0712...`; a merge commit `2c6f4d6e6` was later observed as an ancestor of HEAD, but the result still recorded a land coordination failure.
  - Error detail:
    `land coordination failed: land advancer: restoring refs/heads/main to ba2d3059f14e834dcb147f2a6db3b294d83b0fac after evidence commit failed: git update-ref refs/heads/main: fatal: update_ref failed for ref 'refs/heads/main': cannot lock ref 'refs/heads/main': is at 5a176b332b182f2a959614f63769d835c47ffd97 but expected 2c6f4d6e6961b8a93742cde66bceec056934655a`
  - `cycle_trace[0].final_decision="success"` and `cycle_trace[0].review_result={}`.
- Second attempt result: `.ddx/executions/20260606T142223-94f8f0f8/result.json`.
  - `status=execution_failed`, `outcome=error`.
  - Error detail:
    `land coordination failed: land advancer: landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s: A .ddx/executions/20260606T150832-af40450b/result.json`
  - `cycle_trace[0].final_decision="success"` and `cycle_trace[0].review_result={}`.
- At observation time the worker had claimed it again (`20260606T152024-a21d8c54`) and edited six files in a fresh isolated worktree:
  `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/mod.rs`, `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/tests.rs`, `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/types.rs`, `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/config.rs`, `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/executor.rs`, and `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/tests.rs`.
- Expected DDx behavior: land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures are system or coordination failures, not model-quality failures. The retry policy should reconcile a successfully landed candidate when possible, retry only the land/evidence step when safe, or park/operator-attention the bead when the landing worktree is dirty. It should not blindly rerun implementation at the same or stronger route.

3. Pre-claim intake/system dirt caused repeated warning churn instead of a clear operator lane.
- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-a62d31ce`, title "Publish competitive benchmark artifacts and provenance metadata".
- A live worker repeatedly ran readiness/intake. The log showed readiness timeout after five minutes, then:
  `preserved pre-dispatch implementation changes in /home/erik/Projects/niflheim under refs/ddx/pre-dispatch/20260606T153517-f7a32618; released niflheim-a62d31ce and continuing watch. dirty paths: .ddx/harness-sessions/-2929681.json. recover with git stash apply refs/ddx/pre-dispatch/20260606T153517-f7a32618`
- Bead events included `pre_claim_intake.warn` with timeout/system-unready reason and generic suggested action.
- Expected DDx behavior: repeated same/systemic pre-claim warnings and dirty generated DDx artifacts should become an operator-visible/system-unready state after the configured threshold, not an indefinite poll/release loop.

4. Automatic reviews were not forcing meaningful quality improvement.
- Recent Niflheim "success" attempts had empty review results:
  - `niflheim-9dea95da`, result `.ddx/executions/20260606T043655-845c77ee/result.json`: closed success, `cycle_trace.review_result={}`, `final_decision="success"`, model `gpt-5.4-mini`, result commit `78e51a168...`.
  - `niflheim-da4e1311`, result `.ddx/executions/20260606T085358-d80ada3e/result.json`: closed success, `cycle_trace.review_result={}`, `final_decision="success"`, model `gpt-5.4-mini`, result commit `d43be3a3...`.
  - `niflheim-c75aba30` attempts listed above: `cycle_trace.final_decision="success"` with `review_result={}` despite overall land failures.
- Manual Claude review earlier improved bead wording and completeness, but the automatic worker path did not show enforceable per-AC review evidence.
- Expected DDx behavior: unless review is explicitly skipped with a durable reason (`runtime.NoReview` or a valid `review:skip` + reason policy), an empty review result must be a review error or operator-attention signal. It must not be treated as approval, success, or evidence of quality improvement.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` contains the normative routing table: only `OutcomeGenuineFailure` can escalate; dirty worktree, transport, quota, auth, setup, timeout, and merge-conflict do not escalate. The live Niflheim behavior shows execute-bead/work outcomes are not consistently reduced to this typed table before retry/escalation decisions.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:305-309` wraps `LandingAdvancer` errors as generic `land advancer` errors. The caller can record an overall `execution_failed` without distinguishing landed-candidate reconciliation, ref/index contention, staged evidence dirt, or dirty landing worktree from implementation failure.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1967-2072` emits pre-claim timeout/system-unready outcomes as warn-only by default and continues implementation when strict intake blocking is off. This permits repeated same-fingerprint/system warnings to look like a live worker instead of a system-unready/operator lane.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2175-2315` handles `PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed`, but missing/empty decomposition paths can warn and continue. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3826-3831` can recognize execution-ineligible/decomposed state only after the bead has the right metadata. The Niflheim parent evidence shows structural/decomposed-parent outcomes can still reach repeated implementation attempts and power escalation.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3042-3138` treats success disposition/status as closeable once candidate-cycle review/landing has allowed success, and the old post-merge review is optional. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go:799-823` explicitly proves a worker with `Reviewer: nil` can skip review and close the bead. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:168-193` defines `ReviewResult` with all fields optional except the zero-value enum field; no invariant rejects `{}` as a success-path review artifact.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add compact Niflheim-style regression fixtures under `cli/internal/agent/testdata/niflheim_worker_evidence/` or equivalent. The fixtures should encode the four scenario families above: empty review success, land coordination/ref-lock/staged-evidence failure, decomposed-parent no-changes rationale with child beads, and repeated pre-claim system-unready warning. Do not depend on Niflheim checkout paths.
- Introduce or tighten a single execute-bead retry classification boundary that maps attempt result, land result, review result, no-changes rationale, and pre-claim intake outcome into exactly one action: close, retry same tier, escalate min power/profile, reconcile land/evidence, execution-ineligible/proposed, or operator-attention. Wire this boundary into `ddx work`/`ddx try` so `failclass.Classify` is not merely a standalone helper.
- Enforce review evidence on success. For non-skipped success paths, `cycle_trace.review_result` or the equivalent result field must contain a non-empty verdict, per-AC evidence or documented reason, reviewer routing/session metadata when available, and a classification. `{}` is invalid and must prevent close or produce a review-error/operator-attention event. Explicit review skips remain allowed only when they are durable, reasoned, and visible in result JSON.
- Classify land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures separately from implementation/model failures. If the candidate commit or merge is already landed or is an ancestor of the target, reconcile and close/continue without rerunning implementation. If the landing worktree has staged generated evidence or DDx metadata, retry/preserve the land/evidence step or move to operator attention according to the existing local reliability policy; do not escalate the model for this class.
- Classify decomposed-parent/no-changes rationale as execution-ineligible or operator work before another implementation attempt. Stronger models cannot fix parent beads that are already split into executable children.
- Make worker result JSON and event bodies carry enough audit fields to explain the decision: `failure_class`, `retry_action`, `escalation_count`, requested/actual power/profile/model, review status/verdict, and land/reconcile status.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names. DDx should continue to pass power/profile intent and let Fizeau choose concrete routes.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`; reuse or align with it.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`; this bead requires typed classification and review evidence, not another timeout implementation.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`; this bead uses the Niflheim observation as cross-scenario regression coverage.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond the state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

INTERSECTIONS
- Parent: `ddx-3106674f` (multi-worker hardening and local reliability gates). This bead is the external Niflheim regression/incident closure layer under that reliability epic.
- Dependency: `ddx-2965925e` covers tracker commit index contention. This bead must not reimplement that fix; it must prove land/ref/index/evidence failures classify separately from model failure once the low-level path is hardened.
- Dependency: `ddx-b2846610` covers bounded post-commit review/land. This bead must still require typed review/land outcomes after the timeout wedge is fixed.
- Dependency: `ddx-d67f124e` covers same-fingerprint pre-claim warning escalation. This bead adds the Niflheim `niflheim-a62d31ce` family as regression evidence.
- Related but not a dependency: `ddx-e1a576a7` is an execution-ineligible decomposed parent and a local precedent for the `niflheim-64217cc5` class.
- Related quality framework: `ddx-a9d130d0` and `ddx-ac10e7af` own broader bead lifecycle/operator-lane policy; this bead is concrete worker behavior and evidence gating.","id":"ddx-334c0958","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:iterate","area:agent","area:execute-bead","area:workers","area:review","kind:bug","kind:reliability","severity:critical","spec:FEAT-010","spec:TP-021","adr:024","incident:niflheim-2026-06-06","prevention"],"parent":"ddx-3106674f","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"open","title":"execute-bead worker: gate success on review evidence and classify Niflheim retry failures","updated_at":"2026-06-06T15:55:55.96445459Z"}

2. TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal (internal/registry/resolve_test.go) asserts that when the project copy is absent but the global copy exists, ResolvePlugin returns the global path with layer=="global".
3. Both tests use an isolated temp HOME/XDG fixture and do not touch the real user home.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/registry/... green.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"13eaa6a7758297f2001118d4533a33c70a272e9d","created_at":"2026-06-03T17:21:03.026972073Z","description":"PROBLEM (Principle 5 of epic ddx-602ae31a)
Plugin content can live in two tiers — per-project (ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>) and machine-wide global (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>). Runtime resolution must deterministically prefer the project copy when present, fall back to the global copy, and finally to the baked-in default (ddx plugin only). Without a single authoritative resolver, install/update/init/doctor each reinvent path logic and drift.

ROOT CAUSE
- internal/registry/resolve.go:29 `ResolvePlugin(ctx, projectRoot, name)` is the precedence resolver: project path computed at resolve.go:30, global at resolve.go:35, baked-in fallback at resolve.go:44. It must be the only place precedence is decided and must be covered by named tests so install/init/doctor can rely on it.
- internal/registry/resolve.go:15 `GlobalPluginDir(name)` returns ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name> via ddxroot.GlobalDir(); the project path routes through ddxroot.Path() (in-tree vs convention mode handled there).

PROPOSED FIX
1. Ensure `ResolvePlugin` returns (path, layer, err) with layer ∈ {project, global, baked-in} and the exact precedence project → global → baked-in.
2. Add table-driven tests in internal/registry/resolve_test.go pinning both the prefers-project and falls-back-to-global behaviors against a temp HOME/XDG fixture.
3. Do not alter install/update/init/doctor call sites here beyond confirming they call `ResolvePlugin` (those are covered by sibling children).

NON-SCOPE
- Removing the per-project install tier (both tiers coexist).
- Migrating ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.
- install/update/init/doctor wiring (sibling children).

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — ResolvePlugin routes through both ddxroot.Path() and ddxroot.GlobalDir(); both must be agreed-on before this lands.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS

Operators lack a concise, durable guide for diagnosing a `ddx work --watch` loop that appears alive but does not claim ready beads. The parent incident's suggested action pointed to route/harness configuration even though the actionable fault was DDx's readiness decoder/worker behavior.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The user-facing README is the natural top-level diagnostic surface, but the pre-claim intake contract and silent-idle escalation behavior need to be stated there near the quick-start operational commands in `README.md:40-45`. Worker behavior is implemented in the readiness classification and work loop, but operators should not need to read `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` to know what warn-only, operator-attention, and hard errors mean.

PROPOSED FIX

Parent bead ddx-130cf9ab is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Programmatic decomposition path (the action that fans a too-large bead into children) MUST NOT add the parent as a dep. Existing action templates / system prompts are audited and amended; one golden e2e test (decompose_does_not_self_dep) creates a bead with N children and asserts every child's deps set excludes the parent.
- 'ddx bead doctor' (existing subcommand) gains a check 'parent_ancestor_in_deps' that scans the tracker, lists violating beads, and (with --fix) removes the offending edges. Output is stable / scriptable.

PROPOSED FIX

- cd cli && go test ./... passes.
- lefthook run pre-commit passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. There is no first-time machine setup command, and `ddx init` bundles plugin install with manifest creation, forcing a plugin write into every newly initialized project instead of lazy-resolving from the global tier.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/init.go bundles plugin install with manifest creation; there is no `--global` umbrella path that installs the default `ddx` plugin globally and writes global config.

PROPOSED FIX
1. `ddx init --global` is the umbrella first-time-setup command: installs the default `ddx` plugin globally to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx (via GlobalPluginDir), creates agent-tier links (~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx pointing into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/), and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults.
2. `ddx init` (no flag) creates <project>/.ddx/ and its manifest but does NOT install plugins; plugins are lazy-resolved on first use, preferring the project tier over global per the registry precedence bead.

NON-SCOPE
- Install command `--global` flag (sibling install bead).
- Update command and doctor reporting (sibling update bead).
- The runtime precedence implementation (registry bead).
- Removing the existing per-project install (both modes coexist).
- Migrating legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution).
- Registry resolution sibling bead (GlobalPluginDir) — init --global writes through it.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS
- Epic ddx-602ae31a, Principle 2.","events_attachment":"ddx-3ecfd11d/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-3ecfd11d","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:config","area:cli","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas","kind:feature","phase:3"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-b321fc9b","status":"closed","title":"init: add `ddx init --global` umbrella setup and make plain `ddx init` skip plugin install","updated_at":"2026-06-03T06:52:49.876373361Z"}

There is no signature shape that satisfies AC1 while preserving all current call patterns (Go does not allow same-name method overloads on a single receiver type). 6. Step 0 size envelope is also exceeded for the only resolution that preserves AC1+AC3: the necessary mechanical caller sweep crosses ~170 files in five packages (cmd, internal/agent, internal/server, internal/attemptmetrics, internal/agentmetrics) — well past the "~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages" guardrail. Operator action requested: Re-scope this bead to one of: (a) Add the caller-package migration to scope (delete the NON-SCOPE block and AC4's "no caller-package changes"). This becomes the original TD-027 sweep that previously failed as too large; if pursued, expect a multi-file diff across cli/cmd, cli/internal/agent, cli/internal/server, cli/internal/attemptmetrics, cli/internal/agentmetrics. (b) Replace AC3 with a narrower verification (e.g. "go test ./internal/bead/... and go vet ./internal/bead/... pass"), and explicitly accept that `go build ./...` and lefthook pre-commit will be red until follow-up caller-migration beads land. The follow-up beads would then re-establish AC3 once the sweep is complete. (c) Replace AC1 with an additive shape that does not rename or retype the existing variadic methods — e.g. "add new ctx-first Store methods with distinct names (InitCtx / GetCtx / …) so the legacy variadic surface stays in place during the migration", then a follow-up bead deletes the variadic methods after the caller sweep finishes. This satisfies the spirit of TD-027 (introducing the typed ctx-first surface in the bead package first) without breaking the build outside the package. No code changes were made on this attempt. Request was triaged strictly against the bead body — no /tmp scratch files, no work outside the worktree.

The lifecycle dispatch path still returns the generic `project root mutation rejected` error and the caller path continues to log it as a warning/continuing condition. When that happens in the same run that already exhibits a parent-back-edge state, the operator only sees a soft warning instead of the structural failure that actually occurred.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/lifecycle_dispatch.go:130-165` rejects root mutations but does not include the offending bead ID in the failure shape, and `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:595` plus the surrounding loop code consume the error as a warning path. The result is a muted signal even though the run is already in a bad structural state.

PROPOSED FIX
When the parent-back-edge state and a root-mutation rejection coincide, upgrade the log/event severity from warning to error and attach the offending bead ID to the emitted message. Add the regression `TestDispatchLogsErrorOnRootMutationWithBackEdge` so the severity change cannot drift.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change the store validator, doctor scan/repair, or decomposition prompt text in this bead.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/agent/lifecycle_dispatch.go
- cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
- cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook_test.go

Out-of-scope:
- cli/internal/bead/store.go
- cli/internal/bead/doctor.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go

Add `TestExecuteBeadWorkerCannotMutatePrimaryGitConfig` or equivalent, using a real primary repo plus execute-bead worktree, that contaminates the worker attempt with commands such as `git config --local core.bare true`, `git config --local user.email fixture@ddx.test`, and a nested test subprocess with Git-local env vars set.
2. The test asserts after the attempt that the primary checkout satisfies `git status --short`, has no `core.bare`, has no stray `core.worktree`, and does not contain fixture `user.name`/`user.email`.
3. The fix ensures linked worktree attempts have per-worktree config isolation and/or scrubbed Git env for all agent/test subprocesses that DDx launches.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... ./internal/git/... -run "TestExecuteBeadWorkerCannotMutatePrimaryGitConfig|TestGitFixtureHelpersStripHookEnv|TestCheckGitRepoHealthDetectsCoreBare" -count=1` passes.
5. `sh scripts/git-config-health.sh` passes after the new regression runs.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","created_at":"2026-05-15T05:40:54.944068779Z","description":"PROBLEM
During live `ddx work --watch --harness codex --model gpt-5.4` on bead `ddx-fa6eedcc` at 2026-05-15 05:38 ET, the primary checkout `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx` flipped to `core.bare=true`. `git status --short` in the primary checkout failed with `fatal: this operation must be run in a work tree`; `git config --local --get core.bare` returned `true`. I repaired the local checkout with `git config --unset-all --local core.bare`, but worker attempts must not be able to corrupt the operator's primary repo config.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:391-396` creates linked worktrees with `git worktree add --detach`; `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1242-1247` launches the worker in that isolated worktree. Doctor knows the corruption class at `cli/cmd/doctor.go:1035-1044` (`extensions.worktreeConfig` disabled can corrupt shared config), but this incident occurred while the main repo had `extensions.worktreeConfig=true`, so the execute-bead isolation contract still lacks an end-to-end guard that worker commands and tests cannot mutate the primary checkout's `.git/config` or `.git/config.worktree`.

PROPOSED FIX
Add an execute-bead regression that snapshots the primary checkout git config before a worker attempt, runs a script/agent attempt that intentionally tries to set `core.bare=true` and fixture identity from inside the attempt worktree and test subprocesses, and proves the primary checkout remains a valid worktree with no `core.bare`, no stray `core.worktree`, and no leaked fixture user config after the attempt. Harden the worker environment or worktree setup as needed.

NON-SCOPE
Do not weaken git-config-health hooks. Do not hide the failure by suppressing `git status` errors. Do not rewrite execute-bead history or skip worker isolation; the fix must preserve isolated worktree execution while protecting the primary checkout.","id":"ddx-42a4819d","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:git","area:execution","kind:bug","operator-ux","dirty-tree"],"priority":0,"schema_version":1,"status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: prevent worker attempts from mutating primary git config","updated_at":"2026-05-15T11:57:09.098109835Z"}

2. `TestInitProject_LeavesPluginsForLazyResolution` verifies the initialized project can lazy-resolve the default `ddx` plugin from the global layer through `registry.ResolvePlugin` when the sibling resolver behavior is present.
3. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run 'TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins|TestInitProject_LeavesPluginsForLazyResolution'` passes.
4. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/...` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"3b82c8ae41c24296760ec4c7b1bd41c7bb964c8b","created_at":"2026-06-02T03:19:04.472608585Z","description":"PROBLEM
Plain `ddx init` eagerly installs the default `ddx` plugin into every project. The intended model is that project init only creates `<project>/.ddx/` and the project manifest; plugins should lazy-resolve from the global layer on first use.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/init.go:73 `runInit` currently routes plain init through eager plugin setup.
- cli/cmd/init.go:271-284 calls `registry.InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, defaultplugin.FS(), workingDir)` unconditionally, producing `.ddx/plugins/ddx`, `.agents/skills/ddx`, and `.claude/skills/ddx` in the project.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Remove the default plugin install from the no-flag `ddx init` path.
2. Keep project-local initialization of `<project>/.ddx/` and the manifest intact.
3. Ensure the no-flag path does not create or modify any project `plugins/` tree or agent-tier skill links.
4. Leave actual plugin lookup to `registry.ResolvePlugin`, preferring project over global, from the sibling registry-resolver bead.

NON-SCOPE
- The resolver precedence itself (sibling bead: registry two-layer resolution).
- install/update `--global` wiring (sibling bead).

When pre_claim_intake produces a warn-only rejection with the same fingerprint repeatedly (for example a readiness schema mismatch), the worker logs a warning and goes idle every cycle (pre_claim_intake.warn -> loop.idle). With many ready beads and one daemon, none get claimed and the worker looks alive while making zero progress, leaving the queue idle indefinitely.

ROOT CAUSE
- warn-only policy mode masks the failure: the loop emits pre_claim_intake.warn at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1859, :1982, :2009, :2034, :2073, each carrying a fingerprint computed in cli/internal/agent/readiness_decision.go:23-24, and then goes loop.idle (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1637-1649) with no guard counting consecutive identical fingerprints across distinct bead IDs to break the silent loop.
- The threshold knob exists at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:76-79 (ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.PreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold; zero means DefaultPreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold) and the escalation message format at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1141.

PROPOSED FIX
After N (default DefaultPreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold) consecutive pre-claim warn outcomes sharing the same fingerprint across distinct bead IDs, emit a loop.operator_attention event and a durable operator_attention bead event (reason "preclaim_warn_repeated", carrying fingerprint, count, distinct_bead_ids, example_detail, example_payload), stop before claiming the threshold bead, and leave the remaining ready queue untouched. The threshold is configurable via ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.PreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold; zero falls back to the default.

NON-SCOPE
- Do NOT implement the CI schema drift gate (owned by the sibling child bead).
- Do NOT change verdict decoding or readiness payload schema.
- Do NOT introduce go.mod replace directives.
