First-time machine setup needs an umbrella `ddx init --global` that installs the default ddx plugin globally and wires agent-tier links, while plain `ddx init` must create only the project workspace and lazy-resolve plugins (preferring project over global) rather than eagerly installing. `ddx doctor` must surface both install layers so operators can see where the skill resolves from. The ddx-validate pre-commit hook (ddx-777f4a09, already landed) must keep scoping skill checks to staged skill files regardless of install location.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/init.go:75 dispatches to initGlobal (init.go:318) when --global is set; initGlobal installs the bundled default ddx plugin into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx (init.go:320), creates symlinks ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx into that dir (init.go:336), and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults (init.go:351, writeGlobalConfig init.go:362).
- Plain `ddx init` (no flag) must create <project>/.ddx/ and NOT write plugins/ — plugins lazy-resolve via the registry resolver, preferring the project copy over global.
- cli/cmd/doctor.go:421-448 computes globalPath/projectPath and the projectStatus "lazy-resolves-to-global" (doctor.go:436-444) and prints the "Global Install (...) — <status>" and "Project Install (...) — <status>" lines (doctor.go:447-448).
- Before this, `ddx init` bundled plugin install with manifest creation, there was no global setup command, and doctor reported only a single install layer.

PROPOSED FIX
1. `ddx init --global` installs the default ddx plugin globally, creates ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx links into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx, and writes the global config with convention defaults.
2. `ddx init` (no flag) creates <project>/.ddx/ and does NOT install plugins; first use lazy-resolves from the project layer, falling back to global.
3. `ddx doctor` renders two lines: "Global Install (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx) — ok|missing" and "Project Install (<projectRoot>/plugins/ddx) — ok|missing|lazy-resolves-to-global".
4. The ddx-validate pre-commit hook continues to run skill-related validation only when skill files are staged, regardless of whether the install is global or project-scoped.

NON-SCOPE
- Does NOT remove the per-project install path (both layers coexist).
- Does NOT migrate legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.
- Does NOT add agent-harness skill paths beyond Claude Code and Codex.
- Plugin content writing for non-init installs is owned by the install child beads.

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — global setup routes through ddxroot.GlobalDir(), project init through ddxroot.Path(). Governing epic: ddx-602ae31a (Principle 2). Related: ddx-777f4a09 (ddx-validate hook, landed).","events_attachment":"ddx-4c58ea3e/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-4c58ea3e","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:cli","area:config","area:plugin","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-04beccf3","status":"closed","title":"init/doctor: --global umbrella setup, lazy project init, and two-layer doctor reporting","updated_at":"2026-06-03T02:46:46.787576605Z"}

Runtime plugin lookup must follow a single authoritative precedence — install-in-project (project tier) overrides install-to-agent (global tier), with the embedded default ddx plugin as the last-resort baked-in fallback. Without one resolver, install/update/doctor each reimplement path logic and drift, so a project-local plugin can be silently shadowed by a global copy (or vice-versa).

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/registry/resolve.go:29 `ResolvePlugin(ctx, projectRoot, name)` is the intended single entry point. It checks the project path `ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)/plugins/<name>` (resolve.go:30), then the global path `ddxroot.GlobalDir()/plugins/<name>` (resolve.go:35), then returns layer `"baked-in"` with an empty path only for name=="ddx" (resolve.go:41), else errors (resolve.go:44).
- cli/internal/registry/resolve.go:15 `GlobalPluginDir(name)` defines the global-tier path `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>`.
- The precedence contract is currently under-tested, so regressions in ordering would go undetected.

PROPOSED FIX
- Guarantee `ResolvePlugin` returns layer `"project"` and the project path when the plugin dir exists in BOTH the project and global tiers (project wins).
- Guarantee it returns layer `"global"` and the global path when the project copy is absent but the global copy exists.
- Keep baked-in (empty path, caller uses `defaultplugin.FS()`) valid only for name=="ddx"; return an error for an unknown plugin present in no tier.
- Add table-driven coverage in cli/internal/registry/resolve_test.go using `t.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", ...)` and a temp projectRoot with an in-tree `.ddx/` so both tiers are exercised deterministically.

NON-SCOPE
- Changing how install/update/doctor WRITE plugins (covered by sibling beads).
- Removing the existing per-project install (both modes coexist).
- Migrating any legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution): `ResolvePlugin` routes through `ddxroot.Path()` and `ddxroot.GlobalDir()`; both must be agreed-on. Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- 'ddx bead dep add <bead> <dep>' HARD-REJECTS the edge when <dep> is <bead>'s parent OR any ancestor in the parent chain. Error message names the offending ancestor and prints the relevant parent-chain. Test: ddx_bead_dep_add_rejects_parent_ancestor.
- 'ddx bead create --depends-on X --parent Y' with X == Y (or X an ancestor of Y) HARD-REJECTS at creation time with the same error shape as AC #1. No silent rewrite.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:208:{"acceptance":"1. `ddx init --global` creates `~/.claude/skills/ddx` and `~/.agents/skills/ddx` pointing into `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/` and writes `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml` — TestInitGlobal_CreatesAgentTierLinks.
2. `ddx init` (no flag, in-project) does NOT write to any `plugins/` tree; the plugin lazy-resolves from the global layer — TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins.
3. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... green.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"3946d29110f96ee9d380f6ad28f88ab1fa4fb526","created_at":"2026-06-02T01:50:49.178056222Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-64ecd687","depends_on_id":"ddx-07af62e6","type":"depends_on","created_at":"2026-06-02T03:19:04Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-64ecd687","depends_on_id":"ddx-42c2c630","type":"depends_on","created_at":"2026-06-02T03:19:04Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
First-time per-machine setup has no entry point, and `ddx init` eagerly installs the default ddx plugin into every project. The intended model is: `ddx init --global` does one-time machine setup (global default plugin + agent-tier links + global config), while plain `ddx init` only creates `<project>/.ddx/` and lazily resolves plugins from the global layer on first use.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/init.go:73 `runInit` always installs the default ddx plugin (init.go:271-284 via `registry.InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, defaultplugin.FS(), workingDir)`), producing `.ddx/plugins/ddx`, `.agents/skills/ddx`, `.claude/skills/ddx` unconditionally.
- There is no `--global` flag and no `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml` writer.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add `--global` bool flag to `ddx init` (cli/cmd/init.go).
2. init --global: install the default `ddx` plugin to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx` (via the install-global path from the sibling install bead), create agent-tier links `~/.claude/skills/ddx` and `~/.agents/skills/ddx` pointing into the global plugin dir, and write `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml` with convention defaults.
3. init (no flag): create `<project>/.ddx/` and the manifest, but do NOT install any plugin — plugins lazy-resolve via `registry.ResolvePlugin` (sibling resolver bead), preferring project over global, on first use. Remove the eager install at init.go:271-284 from the no-flag path.

NON-SCOPE
- The resolver precedence itself (sibling bead: registry two-layer resolution).
- install/update `--global` wiring (sibling bead).
- `ddx doctor` reporting and spec/doc updates (sibling bead: doctor + specs).
- Migrating any `~/.config/ddx/` legacy paths.

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution); sibling registry-resolver bead; sibling install/update --global bead (reuse its agent-tier link helper).","events_attachment":"ddx-64ecd687/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-64ecd687","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:plugin","area:cli","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-a08a425b","status":"closed","title":"init: add `ddx init --global` umbrella setup and make plain `ddx init` lazy-resolve plugins","updated_at":"2026-06-02T05:38:52.288275281Z","work-no-changes-count":1}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:210:{"acceptance":"See bead body — 3 verifiable AC items: unit tests covering the four classification scenarios; lefthook green; manual smoke confirming worker emits 'server unreachable' instead of flagging beads when server is down.","created_at":"2026-06-01T01:13:59.24088712Z","description":"**Problem**

When `ddx work --watch` runs against a `ddx server` that is partially
unavailable (e.g. TLS handshake failure, agent-service connection
refusal, persistent route-resolution failures with no candidates), the
worker treats every individual bead as the failure unit. Each attempt
gets flagged with `operator_attention` via the progress-watchdog or
`execution_failed` via the routing preflight, then the next bead is
tried, then the next.

Result: a single server-side problem (one bad TLS cert, an expired
provider credential, a stale model catalog) silently turns the entire
ready queue into "needs human" beads, even though the *beads* are fine
— the *server* is broken. The worker keeps idling, the operator sees
"0 closures in 24h" with no clear signal that this is environmental.

Observed concretely on niflheim on 2026-05-31:

- `ddx server` was serving a TLS cert from
  `$DDX_PROJECT_ROOT/.ddx/server/tls/cert.pem` (Apr 17 fingerprint)
  while the client `ddx work` pinned the cert in `~/.ddx/server/tls/`
  (May 18 fingerprint). The server logged
  `http: TLS handshake error from 127.0.0.1:XXXXX: remote error: tls:
  bad certificate` every ~5 min for ~36 hours.
- Every `niflheim-71365a9f` attempt during that window failed with
  `ResolveRoute: no viable routing candidate: 16 candidates rejected,
  outcome_reason=no_viable_provider`.
- After 15+ attempts the worker flagged the bead for operator
  attention, then quietly idled with `execution-ready=0 blocked=47
  operator-attention=17 needs-human/investigation=10`. No clear
  signal to the operator that the *server* was the cause.

**Desired behavior**

The worker should detect server-level failures separately from
bead-level failures. Two-tier classification:

1. **Bead-level failure** (current behavior): the bead's tests/lint
   fail, the agent commits something invalid, etc. → mark the bead
   `execution_failed`, retry per policy, eventually
   `operator-attention`.
2. **Server-level failure** (new): TLS handshake error, agent-service
   connection refused, persistent `no_viable_provider` across ≥ K
   distinct beads in the last M minutes (default K=3, M=10) → mark
   the *server* unavailable, NOT the individual beads. Loop into a
   probe-and-resume mode: every 30 s ping
   `https://server/api/health`; the moment the server returns 200
   and a small `ddx run --text` smoke test succeeds, re-enter the
   queue. None of the beads attempted during the server-failure
   window get penalized.

The server-failure backoff prevents the per-bead operator-attention
flagging storm we observed. It also makes the actual root cause
visible — `ddx work` should emit a single distinctive log line
("server unreachable: holding queue until /api/health returns") and
expose that state via `ddx work status` so the operator notices it.

**In-scope files (likely; verify before edit)**

- `cli/internal/agent/service_run.go` — wraps `ResolveRoute` →
  classification site for routing failures.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead*.go` — the place where
  `no_viable_provider` is turned into `outcome_reason` for the bead.
- The watcher loop in `cli/internal/work/` (or wherever `ddx work
  --watch` lives) — needs the new server-unavailable state machine.
- `cli/internal/agent/` — health-probe helper.
- New unit tests covering: (a) K consecutive `no_viable_provider`
  across distinct bead IDs trips server-failure mode; (b)
  server-failure backoff doesn't penalize the beads it was about to
  try; (c) successful health probe transitions back to normal queue
  drain; (d) TLS handshake error from the server is classified as
  server-level regardless of bead.

**Acceptance**

1. `go test ./cli/internal/agent/... ./cli/internal/work/...` passes
   with the new tests above.
2. `lefthook run pre-commit` (or equivalent gates) green.
3. Manual smoke: stop `ddx server`, run `ddx work --watch`; observe
   the worker emits "server unreachable" within 30 s and does NOT
   flag any beads for operator attention while the server is down.
   Restart `ddx server`; observe queue drain resumes.

**Out of scope**

- Auto-repairing server-side issues (TLS cert refresh, credential
  rotation). Operator still has to fix the server. The worker just
  has to detect and not amplify the failure.
- Multi-server failover.
- Changing the existing per-bead `operator-attention` semantics —
  those stay for true bead-level failures.

**Severity**: P1. Prevents quiet queue-stall pattern that wastes
operator hours on triage when the actual cause is environmental.","id":"ddx-658cb394","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:work","area:agent","kind:reliability","kind:operator-experience"],"priority":1,"schema_version":1,"status":"open","title":"ddx work: detect server-level failures separately from bead-level (don't flag beads when server is unavailable)","updated_at":"2026-06-06T09:49:52.633763488Z","work-last-detail":"tracker lock timeout (max elapsed, lock: /Users/erik/Projects/ddx/.ddx/.git-tracker.lock, owner pid: 1993005)","work-last-status":"preclaim-hook-failed","work-retry-after":"2026-06-06T09:50:22Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:213:{"acceptance":"1. Agent and Claude execute-bead prompt snapshots no longer instruct `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>`; they instruct parent metadata plus `parent -> child` dependency semantics. Test: TestExecuteBeadPromptSnapshotsUseParentToChildDecompositionEdges.","created_at":"2026-06-07T02:24:01.272784138Z","description":"PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-f3c3b38a is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM: Decomposition can still produce child beads whose deps include the parent when agent-facing instructions or structured decomposition payloads request `child -> parent` edges. That recreates the deadlock even if the direct `dep add` path is fixed only later or if another writer bypasses the CLI.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE: The execute-bead prompt snapshots instruct agents to run `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` at `cli/internal/agent/testdata/execute_bead_prompt_snapshots/agent.txt:18` and `cli/internal/agent/testdata/execute_bead_prompt_snapshots/claude.txt:16`. The preclaim readiness payload model includes suggested child `Parent` and `Deps` fields at `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:259`, but the decomposition application path at `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4678` only creates children and wires parent deps, with no golden assertion that children exclude the parent from deps.

PROPOSED FIX: Update live prompt construction and checked-in prompt snapshots so decomposition instructions create children with `parent=<parent-id>` and wire only `parent -> child` dependencies. Add validation in the programmatic decomposition path that rejects or strips any suggested child dep equal to the parent or an ancestor before tracker mutation. Add a golden e2e test that decomposes a bead into N children and asserts every child has `Parent == parent.ID` and no dependency target equal to the parent.

NON-SCOPE: Do not implement the global `DepAdd` invariant here except where needed by decomposition writer tests. Do not add doctor repair. Do not change post-attempt remediation or operator-attention behavior. Do not relax AC-map completeness rules.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS: `docs/triage/decomposition.md:65` requires child beads to link back via `parent`; `docs/triage/decomposition.md:72` requires dependency edges in the parent-to-child direction; `docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:193` requires executable child beads with dependency edges.

INHERITED DEPENDENCIES: none specified by the parent bead.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Agent and Claude execute-bead prompt snapshots no longer instruct `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>`; they instruct parent metadata plus `parent -> child` dependency semantics. Test: TestExecuteBeadPromptSnapshotsUseParentToChildDecompositionEdges.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.","id":"ddx-66d090f7","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:lifecycle","area:decomposition","kind:safety","kind:correctness","ac-quality:needs-refinement","decomposed"],"parent":"ddx-f3c3b38a","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"status":"open","title":"Stop decomposition writers from creating parent back-edges: part 1","updated_at":"2026-06-07T02:24:01.272784138Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:216:{"acceptance":"1. TestInitGlobal_CreatesAgentTierLinks (cli/cmd/init_test.go or init_functional_test.go) asserts `ddx init --global` makes ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx resolve into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/ and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml.
2. TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins (cli/cmd/init_test.go) asserts plain `ddx init` creates `<project>/.ddx/` and writes nothing under plugins/.
3. 5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"5843edc5ebcb5f64b14cae384b8f22dbb6bdbea5","created_at":"2026-06-03T12:37:10.50344695Z","description":"PROBLEM (epic ddx-602ae31a, Principle 2)

First-time machine setup needs a single umbrella command (`ddx init --global`) that installs the default ddx plugin globally, creates the agent-tier links, and writes the global config. A plain `ddx init` must create the project workspace WITHOUT installing plugins (they lazy-resolve from the global/baked-in layers on first use). `ddx doctor` must surface both install layers so operators can see whether a project install exists or is lazy-resolving to global.

ROOT CAUSE
- `ddx init --global` dispatches to `initGlobal()` (cli/cmd/init.go:75,318), which: creates ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx (init.go:319-323), installs the bundled default plugin there (init.go:328), creates agent-tier links so every harness discovers the skill (init.go:314-316), and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults via `writeGlobalConfig` (init.go:351-366).
- Plain `ddx init` (no flag) must create `<project>/.ddx/` and must NOT write to plugins/ — plugins lazy-resolve via ResolvePlugin (project→global→baked-in).
- `ddx doctor` topology reporting lives at cli/cmd/doctor.go:415 `reportSkillInstallTopology`, which prints `Global Install (<globalPath>) — ok|missing` (doctor.go:447) and `Project Install (<projectPath>) — ok|missing|lazy-resolves-to-global` (doctor.go:448), using `registry.ResolvePlugin` to detect lazy resolution (doctor.go:431-444).

PROPOSED FIX
1. Verify `ddx init --global` creates ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx pointing into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/ and writes the global config.
2. Verify plain `ddx init` creates the project workspace and writes nothing under plugins/.
3. Verify `ddx doctor` renders both the "Global Install" and "Project Install" lines, including the `lazy-resolves-to-global` project status when no project install exists but the global one does.

NON-SCOPE
- Runtime lookup precedence implementation (sibling child — ResolvePlugin).
- The `--global` install/update surface and project install modes (sibling children).
- Removing the existing per-project install entirely (both modes coexist).
- Migrating any ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — initGlobal and doctor topology both route through `ddxroot.GlobalDir()`.","events_attachment":"ddx-67b477d3/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-67b477d3","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:cli","area:config","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-524be1ab","status":"closed","title":"init/doctor: `ddx init --global` umbrella setup, `ddx init` lazy (no plugin install), doctor reports both layers","updated_at":"2026-06-03T16:48:57.361307585Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:218:{"acceptance":"`ddx install --force --local <path>` against a repo containing broken symlinks ONLY under gitignored paths completes successfully. The validation logic either consults .gitignore or skips well-known scratch/dotfile paths.","closing_commit_sha":"08558b9bffef71ec63fdf2485ee4f4d054d8c166","created_at":"2026-05-18T02:12:47.205231666Z","description":"## Summary

Observed against ddx v0.6.2-alpha103-12-ge6379ebc5 on 2026-05-18.

`ddx install <plugin> --force --local <repo>` validates ALL symlinks in the source tree, including those under gitignored paths. Result: any scratch/tmp file with a stale symlink (e.g. files written by a previous `ddx doctor` invocation under doctor/home/.codex/tmp/) breaks the install despite being gitignored and never intended to ship.

## Reproduction

1. In a repo with `doctor/home/.codex/tmp/` gitignored (matches `.codex`)
2. Run any operation that creates broken symlinks under that dir (e.g. an old codex CLI install no longer present)
3. Run `ddx install helix --force --local /path/to/helix`
4. Install fails: `validating package structure: ... broken symlink target ...`

Repro on HELIX:
```
ddx install helix --force --local /home/erik/Projects/helix
# Error: validating package structure:
#   doctor/home/.codex/tmp/arg0/codex-arg0zt0PZe/apply_patch: broken symlink target ...
```

Workaround: rm -rf the offending tmp paths and retry.

## Expected behavior

`ddx install --local` should skip files that are gitignored in the source repository (run via `git check-ignore -v <path>` or read `.gitignore` directly). At minimum, it should skip paths the user could not have intended to ship: well-known scratch/tmp directory names, dotfiles outside the plugin manifest's declared paths, etc.

## Related

- Related to (but distinct from) ddx-efadca32 (lifecycle worktree empty) filed 2026-05-16.
- Blocks HELIX's `ddx install helix --force` for any operator with tmp scratch state present.","events_attachment":"ddx-686564bf/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-686564bf","issue_type":"bug","labels":["kind:bug","area:install","area:validation","observed-failure"],"owner":"easel","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-eec2ad7d","status":"closed","title":"ddx install --local walks gitignored paths and fails validation on broken symlinks in scratch/tmp dirs","updated_at":"2026-05-27T03:18:37.806916401Z"}

cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1263-1315 today reads ReadyExecutionBreakdown into result.NoReadyWorkDetail, emits loop.idle, and either returns (--once / drain) or sleeps (--watch). It never diagnoses or auto-remediates. FEAT-010 §Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation requires the loop to diagnose every non-ready bead, fire matching remediators, and re-scan without sleeping when at least one remediation succeeded.

ROOT CAUSE
No diagnose+dispatch logic exists at the insertion point.

PROPOSED FIX
Inside execute_bead_loop.go, after ReadyExecutionBreakdown populates breakdown (~line 1265) and before applyStop/sleepOrWake (~line 1271), insert the idle-path auto-remediation block.

Pseudo-flow (translate to Go):
1. Build childIndex, openChildIndex, depIndex from the store (these may already be computed inside ReadyExecutionBreakdown; expose them or rebuild as needed).
2. Walk breakdown.Epics ∪ breakdown.DepWaiting ∪ breakdown.OperatorAttention. For each bead, call bead.Diagnose(...) (from cli/internal/bead/diagnose.go).
3. Switch on the BlockReasonCode:
   - superseded_pending_close: queue a no-op (the cascade happens automatically inside any subsequent Close(); to actively trigger it on a known X, call store.Close on the SUPERSEDER Y if it's somehow still open in this branch — typically not; if X is the bead and Y is already closed but the cascade never ran, call a new store.RunSupersededCascade(X, Y) helper that runs the same scope-checked cascade out of band).
   - closure_candidate_misclassified: this should disappear once the RC-1 classifier fix lands; if it still appears, log and surface (defensive).
   - dead_intermediate_all_children_closed: trigger the existing closure-evaluation path against the bead.
   - dead_intermediate_open_children_pending OR epic_of_epic: skip (recursion via children handles it).
   - genuinely_needs_decomposition: queue an AutoDecomposeEpic call. At most ONE auto-decompose dispatch per loop iteration (track a counter). Skip if --no-auto-epic-decompose set.
   - dep_blocked_by, parent_child_state_conflict, claimed_in_progress, provider_route_unavailable, gated_by_budget_or_cooldown, malformed_parent_or_dep_ref, dependency_cycle, closed_or_missing_parent, auto_remediation_exhausted, no_diagnosis: no idle-path action; will surface via ddx work focus.
   - stale_graph_index: trigger an index rebuild and re-diagnose next loop.
4. Execute queued remediations sequentially. Track count of successes.
5. If any remediation succeeded, emit loop.auto_remediated event with {remediation_counts: {...}, total_attempted, total_succeeded}, clearTransientCandidateSkips, and `continue` (re-scan without entering sleepOrWake).
6. If none succeeded, fall through to the existing emit("loop.idle", ...) + sleepOrWake path.

Add operator override flags to cli/cmd/work.go: --no-auto-supersede-close, --no-auto-epic-decompose, --no-auto-closure-reclassify. Plumb them through to the work loop via runtime config (mirror how existing --max-recovery-cost flows in).

Add config key work.autoRemediations: { autoSupersedeClose: bool, autoEpicDecompose: bool, autoClosureReclassify: bool } with defaults of true. Flags override config; per-project config overrides defaults.

Reuse existing cost budget threading: AutoDecomposeEpic counts against --max-recovery-cost just like the existing post-TooLarge decomposer does. Honor SmartRetryCooldown / SetExecutionCooldown precedent for per-bead cooldown after failed auto-decompose (already implemented in the auto_decompose_epic bead).

NON-SCOPE
- Implementing the diagnoser, RC-1 fix, RC-2 cascade, RC-3 walk-up, RC-6 handler — those are separate beads this one depends on.
- Operator surface changes (ddx work focus, ddx work plan) — separate beads.

TestInstallGlobal_WritesToGlobalTreeAndAgentLinks (cli/cmd/install_global_test.go) asserts `ddx install <name> --global --silent` installs to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ and creates a symlink-or-copy under ~/.claude/skills/<name> and ~/.agents/skills/<name>, and records the entry in the global state file only.

6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"4ac5ec29273c18d7542398a829434fd1683b8ee6","created_at":"2026-06-03T12:37:10.38876229Z","description":"PROBLEM (epic ddx-602ae31a, Principle 2)

Operators who run one Claude Code/Codex install per machine need a `--global` surface so skills/personas install once into the machine-wide DDx tree and are discoverable from every project without per-project setup. Today the global install and update paths must write to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ and link into the home-tier agent skill dirs, using a separate global state file so they never touch a project's install.

ROOT CAUSE
- `ddx install` carries the `--global` flag at cli/cmd/install.go:49 and routes through `resolveInstallDirs(global)` (install.go:184) and `selectInstallState(global)` (install.go:202). For global=true, resolveInstallDirs returns installRoot=`ddxroot.GlobalDir()`, agentSkillsDir=`~/.agents/skills`, claudeSkillsDir=`~/.claude/skills` (install.go:185-193); selectInstallState returns `registry.LoadGlobalState`/`SaveGlobalState` (install.go:206-208). Targets are remapped by `adjustInstallTargets`/`remapSkillTargets` (install.go:215,235). Global installs skip the project auto-commit (install.go:174).

        `tracker_lock section=<x> wait=<dur> hold=<dur> retries=<n>`

`ddx update` targets the project path unconditionally with no `--global` surface, `ddx doctor` does not report the global vs project install layers, and the install-topology decision is undocumented.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/update.go targets ddxroot.Path() unconditionally and has no `--global` flag and no in-tree-vs-convention branch.
- ddx doctor has no lines reporting the global tier (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx) or the project tier (ddxroot.Path()/plugins/ddx) install status.
- No ADR or CLAUDE.md text records the install-to-agent vs install-in-project topology and precedence.

After Phase 1 (Foundation `ddx-c6317784`) and per-concern beads (L/C/X/Q/D/A/MIG) land, callers no longer use the removed workflow methods on `*Store` — but they still hold `*Store` as a struct field type for foundational reads/writes/queries/etc. The `Backend` interface composite isn't yet asserted on `*Store`. The concrete types (`*Store`, `RawBackend`, `JSONLBackend`, `ExternalBackend`, `AxonStore`, `*WatcherHub`) live at `cli/internal/bead/` — publicly importable from any other package.

The goal of TD-027 §21 is **physical impossibility** of bypass: not lint-prevented, not review-prevented, but a Go compiler error if any code outside `cli/internal/bead/` tries to import the concrete storage implementations. This bead enforces that via Go's `internal/` rule.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go` — `JSONLBackend` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/backend_external.go` — `ExternalBackend` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go` (or `AxonStore` post `ddx-9c5bca8f`) — public.
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go` — `*WatcherHub` public.
- All supporting concrete implementation files (`claim_liveness.go`, `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc.) — public.

Plus the final assembly step (formerly "K" in earlier breakdowns): `Backend` composite interface declared, `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite, compile-time assertion `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`. These were deferred from Foundation because the composite couldn't be asserted until all per-concern beads removed their methods.

# PROPOSED FIX

This bead does three load-bearing things in one atomic change:

## 1. Narrow caller field types from `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (or narrower)

Every caller file in `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, `cli/internal/server/`, `cli/internal/escalation/`, `cli/internal/agentmetrics/`, `cli/internal/attemptmetrics/`, `cli/internal/processmetrics/`, `cli/internal/exec/`, `cli/internal/agent/try/` that holds `*bead.Store` as a struct field or local variable is updated:

- Most callers: change `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (full interface). Mechanical rename.
- Narrow candidates (identified during per-concern beads): use narrower sub-interface where appropriate (e.g., `cli/cmd/work_focus.go` → `bead.BeadQueries`; `cli/internal/agentmetrics/loader.go` → `bead.BeadReader`).
- Documented exceptions remain on concrete `*Store` access OR on direct file I/O (allowlisted):
  - `cli/cmd/bead_doctor.go` — file-level repair; reads `.ddx/beads.jsonl` directly.
  - `cli/cmd/bead.go` (git auto-commit + conflict-resolution code paths only) — operates on the file as a file.
  - `cli/cmd/sync.go` — `ddx sync` operates on the file.

## 2. Add `Backend` composite + compile-time assertion

- Declare `Backend` as the union of all 11 sub-interfaces:
  `BeadInitializer + BeadReader + BeadLifecycle + BeadEventReader + BeadEventWriter + BeadQueries + BeadDependencyReader + BeadDependencyWriter + BeadArchive + BeadInterchangeReader + BeadInterchangeWriter`.
- Declare `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite (5 reader sub-interfaces) per TD-027 §6.1.
- Add compile-time assertion: `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`.

## 3. Move concretes to internal/

- Create `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` and `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/`.
- Move (with package rename):
  - `store.go` → `internal/storage/store.go` (package `storage`)
  - `backend_jsonl.go` → `internal/storage/backend_jsonl.go`
  - `backend_external.go` → `internal/storage/backend_external.go`
  - `axon_backend.go` / `axon_store.go` → `internal/storage/`
  - `claim_liveness.go` → `internal/storage/`
  - `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc. → `internal/storage/`
  - `watcher.go` → `internal/lifecycle/watcher_hub.go` (package `lifecycle`)
  - `RawBackend` and `OperationApplier` interfaces also move into `internal/storage/`
- Create `cli/internal/bead/factory.go` as the SOLE public construction path:
  ```go
  package bead
  import "cli/internal/bead/internal/storage"
  func NewStore(opts StoreOptions) (Backend, error) { return storage.New(opts) }
  ```
- The public `cli/internal/bead/` package surface is now: `Bead` struct, `Backend` + sub-interfaces, `Operation` + named ops, errors, `IDGenerator` + `ValidateID`, ctx accessors, `LifecycleSubscriber` interface, `Migrator` factory (from MIG bead), and the `ops/` helper sub-packages.

## 4. Lint cleanup

After lockdown, two analyzers in the lint suite (`ddx-e91a45c0`) are redundant because Go's `internal/` rule enforces the same property at the compiler level:
- `concrete-store-methods` — removed.
- `no-internal-store-construction` — removed.

Retained analyzers (defense-in-depth inside `cli/internal/bead/`):
- `direct-bead-jsonl-io` (callers still shouldn't bypass the abstraction)
- `no-new-rawbackend-impls` (new backends use Backend directly)
- `typed-context-accessors-only` (ctx.Value discipline)

# NON-SCOPE
- Per-concern bead work — must complete first.
- AxonStore implementation — `ddx-9c5bca8f`.
- IMP (JSONL→Postgres importer) — separate downstream bead.
- WIRE (real-wire integration tests) — separate downstream bead.

# Acceptance criteria

1. Every caller file in scope (per PROPOSED FIX §1) has its `*bead.Store` field/variable types narrowed to `bead.Backend` or a sub-interface, with the allowlist exceptions (`bead_doctor.go`, file-level paths in `bead.go`, `sync.go`).
2. `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` declares `Backend` as the union of 11 sub-interfaces. `ReadOnlyBackend` declared per TD-027 §6.1.
3. `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` and `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/` exist; all concrete implementation files relocated with appropriate package renames.
4. `cli/internal/bead/factory.go` is the sole public construction path. Exports `NewStore`, `NewLifecycleSubscriber`, `NewMigrator` (from MIG). No other public function constructs a concrete storage type.
5. **Build must succeed**: `cd cli && go build ./...`. **Failure indicates incomplete per-concern bead migration** — diagnose the failing caller and complete its migration before retrying. Do NOT bypass via build tags or temporary public re-exports.
6. Boundary verification test: `TestModuleBoundary_NoInternalImportsOutsideBead` — static analysis scans every Go file under `cli/` outside `cli/internal/bead/` and asserts none of them import `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage` or `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle`. AST-based.
7. Compile-time `var _ Backend = (*storage.Store)(nil)` exists inside `internal/storage/` (verifying Backend satisfaction now that the concrete type is private to that package).
8. Lint cleanup: `concrete-store-methods` and `no-internal-store-construction` analyzers removed from the bead-lints binary. Documented in `cli/tools/lints/README.md`. The retained 3 analyzers stay.
9. Existing tests pass without modification. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

Without `--global`, `ddx install`/`ddx update` must target the active project install location, which differs by mode: in-tree mode (a `<project>/.ddx/` exists) writes under `<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/`; convention mode (no `.ddx/`) writes under `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/`. In both modes the project-scoped agent skill dirs (`<project>/.claude/skills/` and `<project>/.agents/skills/`) must link into whichever plugins/ path the mode resolves to.

ROOT CAUSE
- For global=false, `resolveInstallDirs` (cli/cmd/install.go:184) returns installRoot=`ddxroot.Path(context.Background(), f.WorkingDir)` and skill dirs under `f.WorkingDir/.agents/skills` and `f.WorkingDir/.claude/skills` (install.go:195-197). `ddxroot.Path()` is what selects the in-tree vs convention plugins root, so the install root automatically follows the mode.
- `ddx update` (no flag) must update the project install in whichever mode is active; the non-global path in update.go (dispatched when `opts.Global` is false, update.go:66) operates on the project working dir's resolved root.
- Project installs auto-commit trackable project changes (install.go:174-176) — global installs do not.

PROPOSED FIX

ddx ships both `ddx install <name>` and `ddx plugin install <name>` for the same operation. FEAT-001-cli.md:102 (item 38) names `ddx plugin install <name>` as the canonical surface. The top-level `ddx install` is a vestige that survived the move to project-local plugins (CLAUDE.md:30-33 and engineering-principles.md:18,21 still mention it). Users get duplicate help paths, output messages point at the wrong surface, and the help text itself shows examples that mix both forms.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/cmd/command_factory.go:586 registers `rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newInstallCommand())` (top-level). cli/cmd/install.go:153-163 newPluginCommand reuses the SAME command object via `installCmd := f.newInstallCommand(); installCmd.Use = "install <name>"; cmd.AddCommand(installCmd)`. Both surfaces resolve to the same code; only the help text differs. Output messages and docs that should point users at `ddx plugin install` still point at `ddx install`:
- cli/cmd/list.go:245 — "(none — run 'ddx install <name>' to install)"
- cli/cmd/command_factory.go:464 — "Run 'ddx install %s' to update."
- cli/cmd/doctor.go:839 — "MISSING (%s not found, run: ddx install %s)"
- cli/cmd/init.go:111 — "ddx install helix   - Install HELIX workflow (optional)"
- cli/cmd/install.go:31-34 — Long help body for the cmd (examples list)
- cli/cmd/install.go:974 — error string "run 'ddx install <name> --force' to repair"
- CLAUDE.md:30-33 — mentions `ddx install <name>` as a current surface
- docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:18,21 — same
- docs/helix/01-frame/prd.md:194 — same

Tests that shell `ddx install ...` directly:
- cli/cmd/installation_acceptance_test.go:361,760,870 — exercise `ddx install --offline ...` (verify behavior; rewrite to `ddx plugin install --offline` if --offline is preserved)
- cli/cmd/install_manifest_test.go:668 — asserts the removed `--global` flag still errors (rewrite to call `ddx plugin install --global` and assert the same error)

PROPOSED FIX
Single-PR change:
1. Remove `rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newInstallCommand())` at cli/cmd/command_factory.go:586. Keep newInstallCommand() the constructor since cli/cmd/install.go:159 newPluginCommand still calls it. Optionally rename to newPluginInstallCommand for clarity.
2. Update newPluginCommand at cli/cmd/install.go:153-163 to also override the Long help body so it no longer references "ddx install" examples (it currently inherits the parent Long with mixed examples).
3. Update the 4 user-facing output messages in cli/cmd/{list.go:245, command_factory.go:464, doctor.go:839, init.go:111, install.go:974} to say `ddx plugin install ...`.
4. Update docs that name `ddx install` as a current surface: CLAUDE.md:30-33, docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:18,21, docs/helix/01-frame/prd.md:194. Replace with `ddx plugin install`.
5. Update the two tests cited above to invoke `ddx plugin install ...`. Add a new TestDDxInstall_TopLevelCommandRetired that runs `ddx install whatever` and asserts an exit code != 0 with an error message pointing to `ddx plugin install`.
6. Run `make build && ddx --help` and verify `install` is no longer listed under top-level commands. Verify `ddx install foo` produces the typical cobra "unknown command" error.

NON-SCOPE
- `ddx installed` and `ddx uninstall` (cli/cmd/command_factory.go:587-588) are also vestiges per FEAT-001-cli.md:107 (item 42 says they were removed but they are still registered). File a separate follow-up bead for those; this bead is scoped strictly to `ddx install`.
- The --offline flag semantics, the --force flag, or any other install behavior. Only the command surface moves.
- Library-registry or plugin manifest changes.

PARENT AND DEPS
No parent. `ddx work --watch` can appear healthy while repeatedly skipping claims on a ready queue. A top-level operator-visible signal is needed when the worker's recent claim success rate drops to zero or below a configured threshold over a rolling window.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The work loop owns claim-attempt telemetry and operator-visible events. Runtime configuration for claim throughput belongs with `ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime` in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:86-92`, and operator-visible loop events are emitted from the loop. Without a rolling claim-success-rate guard, repeated non-successes only appear as low-level intake warnings or idle events instead of a clear stuck-worker signal.

PROPOSED FIX
Track claim-attempt outcomes in a rolling window, default window=10 and threshold=0.0, and emit a single `loop.operator_attention` event plus stderr/log text when the full window is at or below threshold. Reset the warned state after a successful claim so a later regression can signal again. Expose CLI knobs on `ddx work --watch` for window and threshold.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change verdict decoding, same-fingerprint escalation, shared schema files, README documentation beyond CLI help if needed, Fizeau route/harness configuration, lock handling from ddx-57c40485, or route-resolution wedge handling from ddx-8f2e0ebf. Do not make low claim rate close, park, or mutate individual beads by itself. Parent lifecycle, child dependency wiring, and tracker mutation are orchestrator-owned.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT / CROSS-REF
AR-2026-05-17 follow-up context; ADR-023 readiness warn-only policy; adjacent reliability bugs ddx-57c40485 and ddx-8f2e0ebf are cross-references only.

DEPENDENCIES

`~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-<bead>-<attempt>/` per attempt
(FEAT-010 layer 2). For projects with native build toolchains (Rust + cargo,
JVM + gradle, Go + module cache, etc.), the fresh worktree starts with an
empty toolchain build cache, so the agent's first build inside is a cold
build from scratch. FEAT-010 §Layer 2 and TD-010 §Storage Model are silent
on this — they specify worktree creation and evidence capture but make no
statement about toolchain build-cache sharing or per-worktree I/O budgets.

OBSERVED COST (2026-05-28, niflheim project):
- A single in-flight `ddx try` attempt accumulated `target/` = 14 GB in the
  execute-bead worktree.
- Two concurrent workers + the project's own niflheim recovery run
  collectively wrote ~23 GB to the same SSD over ~30 minutes.
- The project's WAL recovery (read-bound, ~2 MB written) was visibly
  starved by worker cargo build I/O.
- Each fresh attempt repeats this cost — the worktree is destroyed at
  attempt end (or preserved with `--no-merge`), so the cache is lost.

ROOT CAUSE (spec-level): FEAT-010 treats worktree resource cost as a
non-concern. The feature explicitly lists "workspace creation, bead
attempt, merge or preserve, evidence bundle" but lists no resource-budget
or cache-reuse hook surface. There is no ADR governing per-worktree I/O,
no documented hook for projects to opt into shared toolchain caches, and
no operator-visible counter that would surface this contention in
`ddx work status` or `ddx doctor`.

PROPOSED EVOLUTION (the executing agent should choose how to slot these
into the artifact stack; below is the recommended shape):

1. **Amend FEAT-010 §Layer 2** (`docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md`):
   - Add a "Worktree resource expectations" subsection acknowledging that
     execute-bead worktrees do not, by default, share native toolchain
     build caches with the parent checkout or with sibling attempts.
   - State the operator's responsibility (set per-project env like
     `CARGO_TARGET_DIR`, `GRADLE_USER_HOME`, `GOPATH/pkg/mod`, etc., or
     project-level config) versus DDx's responsibility (pass through
     environment unchanged, do not invent toolchain-specific knobs).
   - Document that `ddx work` makes no concurrency guarantee for native
     build steps inside attempts; concurrent workers may contend.

2. **Amend TD-010** (`docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-010-executions.md`):
   - Add a §Worktree environment passthrough subsection naming
     `CARGO_TARGET_DIR`, `GRADLE_USER_HOME`, `MAVEN_OPTS`, `GOPATH`,
     `GOMODCACHE`, `npm_config_cache`, `PIP_CACHE_DIR`, etc. as
     environment variables that flow unmodified from the `ddx try`
     caller into the agent process inside the worktree.
   - Specify the invariant: DDx never sets, overrides, or unsets any
     toolchain cache env var. Operator/project owns the policy.

3. **Optionally add an ADR** (e.g., `ADR-027-worktree-resource-isolation.md`)
   recording the decision to keep build-cache policy outside DDx, with the
   rationale (cross-language, single-responsibility, prevents lock-in to a
   specific toolchain). If the executing agent decides one ADR is overkill,
   adding the same paragraph as a §Rationale block at the end of TD-010 is
   acceptable.

4. **Update CLAUDE.md "Execution Evidence Convention" section** if needed
   to cross-reference the new TD-010 environment-passthrough subsection
   so agents working in execute-bead worktrees know they may set
   `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` (or equivalent) per project policy.

NON-SCOPE:
- Implementing build-cache mounting, sccache integration, or any
  toolchain-specific knob inside ddx itself. This bead is documentation
  only — it locks down the boundary so future implementation work has
  a clear policy to refer to.
- Changing `ddx try` worktree creation semantics or evidence capture.
- Changing the worktree path under `~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/`.
- Adding `ddx doctor` or `ddx work status` instrumentation for disk
  contention (potential follow-up bead, not this one).
- Project-side fixes (niflheim has its own bead niflheim-e5cd7c69 for
  the cargo-target-dir workflow documentation).

IN-SCOPE FILES:
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-010-executions.md
- Optionally: docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-worktree-resource-isolation.md
  (new) — only if the agent chooses the ADR path over TD-010 §Rationale.
- Optionally: CLAUDE.md (cross-reference paragraph only, no behavior change).

OUT-OF-SCOPE FILES:
- Any cli/ code — this is a docs-only evolution.
- Other FEAT/TD/ADR documents — touch only the listed artifacts unless

Worker 1 ran ddx-69a9ecd3 to completion (closed, 1h11m) but its durable-audit commit was SIGKILLed and the worker exited "operator attention required", leaving .ddx/beads.jsonl, .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl, .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl, .ddx/attachments dirty. Memory was healthy (31Gi free) — NOT OOM. Error: `commit durable audit outputs: committing durable audit outputs: : signal: killed`.

ROOT CAUSE: cli/internal/agent/durable_audit.go:88 wraps the WHOLE commit body (status/add/diff/commit) in context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second); that ctx flows to exec.CommandContext (cli/internal/git/exec.go:38). While the git commit was blocked behind the concurrent worker's hold on the shared main-git lock (.ddx/.git-tracker.lock via withTrackerLock, tracker_lock.go:133-152) / .git/index.lock, the 30s deadline fired and Go sent SIGKILL to git -> CombinedOutput() returns empty output + *exec.ExitError("signal: killed") (durable_audit.go:125-127). The classifier isTransientGitContention (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1026-1041) matches index.lock / "unable to write new index file" / "cannot lock ref" / "unable to update the ref" but NOT "signal: killed"/context-deadline, so finalizeDurableAuditOrStop (execute_bead_loop.go:1311-1336) sets OperatorAttention and exits. The commit is ALREADY idempotent on re-entry (durable_audit.go:98-122), so a retry would succeed — only the misclassification-driven EXIT blocks the idempotent re-run.

PROPOSED FIX: Make isTransientGitContention (execute_bead_loop.go:1026) a SUPERSET that returns true for: (a) errors.Is(err, context.DeadlineExceeded) and substrings "signal: killed" and "context deadline exceeded" (the SIGKILL/deadline class — defense-in-depth since the exec.ExitError from a ctx kill may not retrievably wrap context.DeadlineExceeded), AND (b) the "tracker lock timeout (...)" string from tracker_lock.go:283 (the lock-acquire-wait-exhausted class). This SUPERSEDES ddx-2189a07f (which adds only the tracker-lock-timeout string and is currently wedging on a routing issue) — folding both transient forms into one coherent classifier change avoids two beads racing to edit the same function. Keep all existing matches.

NON-SCOPE: changing the 30s deadline value or decoupling it from the lock wait (that is the sibling bead B2); worktree-leak cleanup (ddx-274943fc); classifier call sites other than the durable-audit branch.
IN-SCOPE FILES: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (classifier), cli/internal/agent/durable_audit_test.go (extend the transient table ~lines 175-185 via runAuditWithFinalizeErr).","events_attachment":"ddx-83361480/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-83361480","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:execution","kind:bug","kind:reliability","phase:iterate","spec:FEAT-010","spec:TP-021","prevention:ddx-23ac2796","supersedes:ddx-2189a07f"],"parent":"ddx-3106674f","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-2bebdf9d","status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: classify SIGKILL/context-deadline durable-audit commit (signal: killed) + tracker-lock-timeout as transient, not operator-attention","updated_at":"2026-06-06T07:42:44.170267001Z"}

Parent bead ddx-937de9fc is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 4 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Skills, personas, and plugin content need to install in two distinct locations depending on operator intent:

- **install-to-agent (global)**: written under the user's agent-tier paths (e.g. ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, or the equivalent each harness reads). Skills and personas are then available across every project on the machine without per-project setup. Lives under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ with symlinks/copies into the agent-tier paths.
- **install-in-project**: written under ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/. In in-tree mode that is <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/; in convention mode that is ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/. Either way the project-scoped agent paths point into it (e.g. <project>/.claude/skills/ link target).

Today `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, and `ddx init` all assume per-project installs only. There is no `--global` surface. Operators who want a single Claude Code install per machine (the common case) must repeat the install for every project.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go and cli/cmd/update.go target ddxroot.Path() unconditionally.
- cli/cmd/init.go bundles plugin install with manifest creation.
- The agent-tier paths (~/.claude/skills/...) are written only when the operator manually links them, or via the legacy install paths that were retired (per the user's `feedback_no_local_replace` and the home-directory-install retirement noted in CLAUDE.md).

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add `--global` flag to `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, `ddx plugin show`. When set:
   - Resolve target: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/.
   - Write the plugin content there.
   - Create symlinks (or copies, OS-dependent) into the agent-tier skill paths the user's agents read: ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, and any others the agent registry knows about.
   - Skill discovery from a fresh shell finds the skill via the agent-tier path.

2. Without `--global`, `ddx install` writes to ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/ and links into <project>/.claude/skills/<name> AND <project>/.agents/skills/<name>. In convention mode (no <project>/.ddx/) the project's agent-tier paths still point into the XDG project dir's plugins. In in-tree mode they point into <project>/.ddx/plugins/.

3. `ddx init --global` is
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- TestUpdateGlobal_UpdatesGlobalNotProject asserts `ddx update --global` updates only the global plugin tree.
- TestUpdate_RespectsConventionVsInTreeMode asserts `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active.
- ddx doctor's two new lines ("Global Install" and "Project Install") render correctly.

Parent bead ddx-c191e91a is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
DDx-managed filesystem state accumulates indefinitely. On the Fizeau project (sibling repo, /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau) on 2026-05-14:
- /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/ contained 9,735 inodes across ~9700+ directories. Many were .execute-bead-wt-* worktrees dating back to 2026-05-07 (a week stale). Each is a full repo checkout, multiplying repo size on disk.
- .ddx/workers/ held 35 agent-loop-<nano-id>/ directories. Most show last_activity_at older than 1 hour with no live process. They keep persisting per-attempt status.json entries.
- .ddx/executions/ held 44 attempt directories. Some have unmerged result.json state (a previous merge or worker crash left them in DU). No automatic GC.
- .ddx/attachments/ holds 355 per-bead artifact directories. They flood `git status` as untracked since nothing gitignores them.

ROOT CAUSE
- `ddx try` creates a worktree under `/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-<bead>-<timestamp>-<hash>/` and only sometimes cleans up. Failure paths (worktree_lost, execution_failed, process crash) consistently leave the worktree behind. There's no startup reaper for stale worktrees.
- `ddx work` and `ddx try` write a worker status.json under .ddx/workers/agent-loop-<nano-id>/ and the writer is responsible for cleanup. Process crashes leave the directory behind; no startup reaper claims them.
- .ddx/executions/<timestamp>/ is meant to be retained as evidence (per CONTRACT-003 / FEAT-010 evidence pile). But there's no retention policy: directories accumulate forever. Some land in unmerged git state after partial commits.
- .ddx/attachments/ and .ddx/run-state*.json are written by every ddx invocation, but the project's .gitignore policy for them is inconsistent (.ddx/executions is ignored; .ddx/attachments and .ddx/run-state.json appear in `git status` constantly).

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add a startup reaper to `ddx work` and `ddx try`: on launch, scan /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/, identify worktrees whose parent ddx-try/ddx-work process is dead (no PID match), `git worktree remove` + `rm -rf` them. Bound the scan to N=200 per launch so a cold start doesn't take forever. Configurable via DDX_WORKTREE_REAP_MAX_AGE (default 72h).
2. Add a startup reaper for `.ddx/workers/agent-loop-*/`: read status.json's `pid` and `last_activity_at`; if pid is gone OR activity > 30 min stale, remove the directory.
3. Add a configurable execution-evidence retention policy:
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- TestWorkStartup_ReapsStaleWorkerDirs asserts that .ddx/workers/agent-loop-*/ directories whose status.json shows pid=dead or last_activity_at > 30 min stale are removed at startup.
- TestExecutionsRetention_ArchivesOldEvidence asserts that .ddx/executions/<timestamp>/ entries older than DDX_EXECUTION_RETAIN_DAYS are moved under .ddx/executions-archive/<year>/<month>/ (or deleted if DDX_EXECUTION_RETAIN_DAYS=0).
- `ddx doctor` reports the count of stale worktrees, worker dirs, and execution dirs without making changes. `ddx doctor --apply` runs the reaper paths.

2. `TestDefaultVerificationCommandRunnerAllowsConfiguredLongGate` or equivalent proves the verification timeout is configurable/worker-derived and can run a command longer than 60s without forcing `no_changes_unverified`.
3. `TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesVerifiedLongCommandCloses` proves a `verification_command` that runs longer than the old 60s limit but exits 0 closes the bead as `already_satisfied` and emits `no_changes_verified`.
4. `TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesVerificationTimeoutKeepsOpenAndReaps` proves timeout keeps the bead open with `triage:no-changes-unverified`, records timeout evidence, and leaves no verification process tree behind.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'Test(DefaultVerificationCommandRunner|ExecuteBeadWorkerNoChanges.*Verification)' -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"f609f22ef84a0d7b05ba89dd3e9fd5b92fbfa446","created_at":"2026-05-15T13:09:07.942514913Z","description":"PROBLEM
A `no_changes_rationale.txt` with `verification_command:` can time out, leave its child process tree running, and let `ddx work --watch` move on to another bead while the old verification is still consuming the project. Live reproduction on 2026-05-15 in `/Users/erik/Projects/fizeau`: `fizeau-bc9586d7` returned `verification_command: sh -lc "go test ... && lefthook run pre-commit"`; the watcher logged `• fizeau-bc9586d7 → no_changes` and started `fizeau-7af3f548` at 13:07:21Z, while `ps` still showed orphaned PID 3510889 (`sh -lc go test ...`) and child PID 3512539 (`go test ./... -count=1`) under PPID 1.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:117-128` hard-codes `DefaultVerificationCommandTimeout = 60 * time.Second` and runs the command with `exec.CommandContext(cctx, "/bin/sh", "-c", command)`. `exec.CommandContext` kills only the direct shell on timeout; because workers commonly emit nested shell commands such as `sh -lc "go test ..."`, the actual test process survives as an orphan. The 60s timeout is also shorter than normal bead acceptance gates like `go test ./...` plus `lefthook run pre-commit`, so legitimate already-satisfied checks are classified as unverified/no-progress instead of closing the bead.

PROPOSED FIX
Run no_changes verification commands with the same process-tree discipline used by agent executors: start the shell in its own process group on Unix, terminate the group on timeout/cancel, and wait/reap before returning. Replace the hard-coded 60s ceiling with a worker/config-derived timeout that is long enough for normal acceptance gates, or make it configurable with a conservative default. Preserve the contract: exit 0 closes as `already_satisfied`; non-zero/timeout keeps the bead open with clear `no_changes_unverified` evidence.

NON-SCOPE
Do not weaken no_changes verification semantics. Do not let verification run asynchronously. Do not close a bead without a passing command. Do not change readiness classification or route selection.","events_attachment":"ddx-8a0aea8e/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-8a0aea8e","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:work","kind:bug","operator-ux","downstream:fizeau","dirty-tree"],"priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-af6f8fc7","status":"closed","title":"work: reap and correctly budget no_changes verification commands","updated_at":"2026-05-15T15:59:18.191703305Z"}

Parent bead ddx-130cf9ab is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Programmatic decomposition path (the action that fans a too-large bead into children) MUST NOT add the parent as a dep. Existing action templates / system prompts are audited and amended; one golden e2e test (decompose_does_not_self_dep) creates a bead with N children and asserts every child's deps set excludes the parent.
- 'ddx bead doctor' (existing subcommand) gains a check 'parent_ancestor_in_deps' that scans the tracker, lists violating beads, and (with --fix) removes the offending edges. Output is stable / scriptable.
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- 'ddx bead doctor' (existing subcommand) gains a check 'parent_ancestor_in_deps' that scans the tracker, lists violating beads, and (with --fix) removes the offending edges. Output is stable / scriptable. Test: ddx_doctor_detects_and_repairs_back_edge.
- ddx work no-progress remediation: when an attempted bead's outcome is 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale' AND any freshly-created child carries the attempted bead in its deps, ddx auto-emits an 'operator_attention' event with reason 'parent_back_edge' and stops re-claiming that bead. (Today it loops indefinitely.) Test: ddx_work_halts_on_parent_back_edge.

Per-pool in-flight max-attempt cap enforced (test simulates concurrent genuine failures, asserts cap). 4. quota-exhausted -> global pool cooldown, not N per-bead mutations (test). 5. cd /Users/erik/Projects/ddx/cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green. 6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"65f0da163dbdd3845b28118fbc84fc0a965bfa2d","created_at":"2026-05-31T15:22:49.608697929Z","description":"Implement the ddx side of the FEAT-004 failure-classification table: ddx work maps each attempt outcome to exactly the action in the addendum table (success/already_satisfied=close; no_changes=close/unclaim NO tier-escalation; genuine impl/capability failure=escalate middle->max ONCE; dirty=clean+same-tier retry; transport=same-tier reroute; quota-exhausted=global pool cooldown; auth/setup=operator-attention; timeout=clean+retry then operator-attention; merge-conflict=unclaim+retry; max genuine failure=operator-attention). ddx owns the ONE middle->max tier escalation (per the escalation-owner decision bead fizeau-994604ec); a per-pool in-flight max-attempt cap prevents multi-worker stampede. Depends on the escalation-owner contract + the shared classifier (fizeau-44e98499).","events_attachment":"ddx-8d6af771/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-8d6af771","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:execute-bead","area:workers","kind:feature","spec:FEAT-004","phase:1","triage:no-changes-unjustified"],"notes":"SELF-CONTAINED SPEC (the ddx-side FEAT-004 here is Beads; the ROUTING policy lives in the fizeau repo, NOT accessible to a cold ddx agent — so the normative failure-classification table is inlined below; implement against THIS, do not look for a ddx FEAT-004 routing addendum):

Map each ddx-work attempt outcome to exactly one action:
- success / already_satisfied -> close bead
- no_changes (agent produced nothing + rationale) -> close or unclaim per rationale; do NOT escalate tier
- genuine implementation/capability failure (tests fail after real work; AC unmet) -> escalate middle->max ONCE
- dirty worktree / partial write -> clean/reset worktree, same-tier retry (not escalation)
- provider transport failure (i/o timeout, conn refused, 5xx) -> same-tier reroute to another dispatchable candidate; no tier change
- quota exhausted (retry_after) -> GLOBAL pool cooldown/sleep until reset; NOT per-bead mutation, not escalation
- auth/config missing, toolchain/setup failure -> operator-attention (typed bead state); do not escalate
- timeout / no-progress watchdog -> clean + same-tier retry once, then operator-attention
- merge/land conflict -> unclaim + retry later; not escalation
- max-tier genuine failure -> operator-attention (stop escalating)

operator-attention = explicit bead state transition (not a notification/indefinite wait). The single middle->max escalation owner is ddx work; a per-pool in-flight max-attempt cap prevents multi-worker stampede. Scope tests to avoid hangs (the prior attempt hit a 3m test timeout — bound any test that spawns/loops).","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-f5db2b63","spec-id":"FEAT-004","status":"closed","title":"routing P1: ddx applies shared failure-classification table; single-owner one-hop middle->max escalation","updated_at":"2026-05-31T20:49:57.479846861Z","work-no-changes-count":1}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:317:{"acceptance":"1. cli/internal/agent/work/stop.go defines StopCondition enum: Drained | Once | Signal | Budget | NoProgress | Blocked, and drain.stop(state) is the only production budget/terminal stop classifier.
2. Cost-cap checks are removed from executor closure call sites and reviewer group cost is charged to the same budget accumulator before close eligibility is evaluated.
3. No-progress retry budget is incremented only for valid implementation attempts; TestStopCondition_NoProgress_IgnoresIntakeRoutingReviewAndOperatorStates covers intake block, decomposition, claim race, routing preflight, quota/transport/auth/tool setup, review-error, and needs_human cases.
4. Review REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK no longer reopens a closed bead. A review_fixable_gap prevents close, records review-fixable-gap, and schedules one bounded repair cycle with repair_context_from_review_group. TestPreCloseReviewFixableGap_PreventsCloseAndSchedulesRepair covers this.
5. review_spec_gap, review_missing_acceptance, review_too_large at depth cap, review_unsafe_or_out_of_scope, and exhausted review_error transition to blocked with needs_human and do not consume no-progress budget. TestReviewTerminalClassifications_BlockWithoutNoProgress covers this.
6. MinPower retry/escalation behavior from ddx-cfedee8e is wired in the attempt/retry path without mutating explicit harness/model/profile pins. TestRetryPolicy_ReviewFixableGapRaisesMinPowerAndPreservesPins covers the path.
7. Execution records include append-only cycle trace data for initial and repair cycles: cycle_index, attempt_id, result_rev, implementer route facts, review_group_id, reviewer_index values, review verdicts, and final decision. TestExecutionTrace_RecordsInitialAndRepairCycles covers both cycles.
8. Downstream-consumer audit for cost-cap and stop-condition event names is committed to .ddx/executions/<run-id>/cost-cap-consumers.md.
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
10. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:02:28.321106862Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-9228a484","depends_on_id":"ddx-848069a3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:02:28Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-9228a484","depends_on_id":"ddx-a8718bec","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:02:28Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-9228a484","depends_on_id":"ddx-dc157075","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:02:28Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-9228a484","depends_on_id":"ddx-09d2990c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:15:17Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-9228a484","depends_on_id":"ddx-99a3be32","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-06T15:17:58Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-9228a484","depends_on_id":"ddx-7e1fed08","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-07T16:13:58Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
The execution loop now needs a single durable stop/retry policy for the reliable factory workflow. Current code still exposes older post-merge review/reopen semantics and scattered stop logic: ExecuteBeadReport.ReviewVerdict is documented as post-merge in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:226, Reopen is documented for REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:300, no-progress suppression only excludes a narrow transient set at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1576, and C9 previously depended on ddx-cfedee8e even though C9 absorbs that scope.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1722 and cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1818 contain historical cost-cap stop sites outside a first-class StopCondition gate.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:226 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:300 still describe post-merge reviewer close/reopen behavior, which conflicts with ADR-024 default adversarial pre-close review.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1576 counts no-progress by base/result equality but only excludes transport/quota/routing/timeout/merge_conflict outcome reasons; intake blocks, decomposition, review errors, and operator-required states must not consume no-progress retry budget.
- ddx-9228a484 previously listed ddx-cfedee8e as a dependency while also absorbing it, creating a queue deadlock.

PROPOSED FIX
- Implement cli/internal/agent/work/stop.go with StopCondition values Drained, Once, Signal, Budget, NoProgress, Blocked.
- Move all budget/cost-cap decisions, including implementer plus adversarial reviewer group cost, into drain.stop(state).
- Relocate MinPower retry behavior from ddx-cfedee8e into the attempt/retry path without mutating requested harness/model/profile pins. A review_fixable_gap may raise MinPower for the repair attempt; spec gaps, missing acceptance, too-large, unsafe/out-of-scope, review errors beyond cap, intake ambiguity, and decomposition overflow block for the operator instead.
- Make no-progress budget count only valid implementation attempts where checkout/context existed and the implementer had an opportunity to modify or verify code. Exclude intake rewrites/blocks/decomposition, claim races, routing preflight, quota/transport/auth/tool setup, review errors, and operator-action states.
- Keep append-only cycle trace fields for every implementation cycle and repair cycle: cycle_index, attempt_id, result_rev, implementer route facts, review_group_id, reviewer slots, review result, final decision.

NON-SCOPE
- Implementing the adversarial review gate itself; that is tracked separately.
- Frontend display of stop reasons.
- Poll-interval defaults and idle workers; already handled by ddx-dc157075 / C10.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"codex","resolved_model":"gpt-5.4-mini","fallback_chain":[],"actual_power":8}","created_at":"2026-05-06T09:57:33.739548542Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=codex model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260506T095557-abbe996b","harness":"codex","model":"gpt-5.4-mini","input_tokens":1639001,"output_tokens":9876,"total_tokens":1648877,"cost_usd":0,"duration_ms":93966,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"2026-05-06T09:57:33.982549382Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=1648877 model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"erik","body":"This bead still spans multiple distinct implementation slices that are not safely landable as one execute-pass at the current decomposition depth: stop-condition classification and cost-cap routing, no-progress accounting, pre-close review repair-cycle orchestration, execution trace schema, and the associated regression tests. .ddx/beads.jsonl:320:{"acceptance":"1. `ddx install <name> --global --silent` installs the plugin to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ and creates a symlink or copy under ~/.claude/skills/<name> and ~/.agents/skills/<name>.
2. `ddx install <name>` (no --global, in-tree mode) installs to <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/ and creates project-tier links under <project>/.claude/skills/<name> and <project>/.agents/skills/<name>.
3. `ddx install <name>` (no --global, convention mode) installs to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/ and creates project-tier links pointing into that XDG plugins/ path.
4. TestPluginLookup_PrefersProjectOverGlobal asserts that when both paths have the plugin, the project copy wins.
5. TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal asserts that when the project copy is missing, the global copy is used.
6. TestInitGlobal_CreatesAgentTierLinks asserts ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx point into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/.
7. TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins asserts `ddx init` (in-project) does not write to plugins/ — plugins lazy-resolve from global.
8. TestUpdateGlobal_UpdatesGlobalNotProject asserts `ddx update --global` updates only the global plugin tree.
9. TestUpdate_RespectsConventionVsInTreeMode asserts `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active.
10. ddx doctor's two new lines ("Global Install" and "Project Install") render correctly.
11. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/registry/... green.
12. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
13.
