fc","depends_on_id":"ddx-6a23cbd3","type":"depends_on","created_at":"2026-06-03T21:49:46Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-937de9fc","depends_on_id":"ddx-fab52595","type":"depends_on","created_at":"2026-06-03T23:07:18Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-937de9fc","depends_on_id":"ddx-b418c28c","type":"depends_on","created_at":"2026-06-03T23:07:18Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-937de9fc","depends_on_id":"ddx-c0292631","type":"depends_on","created_at":"2026-06-03T23:07:18Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-937de9fc","depends_on_id":"ddx-7e585c0a","type":"depends_on","created_at":"2026-06-03T23:07:18Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Skills, personas, and plugin content need to install in two distinct locations depending on operator intent:

- **install-to-agent (global)**: written under the user's agent-tier paths (e.g. ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, or the equivalent each harness reads). Skills and personas are then available across every project on the machine without per-project setup. Lives under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ with symlinks/copies into the agent-tier paths.
- **install-in-project**: written under ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/. In in-tree mode that is <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/; in convention mode that is ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/. Either way the project-scoped agent paths point into it (e.g. <project>/.claude/skills/ link target).

Today `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, and `ddx init` all assume per-project installs only. There is no `--global` surface. Operators who want a single Claude Code install per machine (the common case) must repeat the install for every project.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go and cli/cmd/update.go target ddxroot.Path() unconditionally.
- cli/cmd/init.go bundles plugin install with manifest creation.
- The agent-tier paths (~/.claude/skills/...) are written only when the operator manually links them, or via the legacy install paths that were retired (per the user's `feedback_no_local_replace` and the home-directory-install retirement noted in CLAUDE.md).

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add `--global` flag to `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, `ddx plugin show`. When set:
   - Resolve target: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/.
   - Write the plugin content there.
   - Create symlinks (or copies, OS-dependent) into the agent-tier skill paths the user's agents read: ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, and any others the agent registry knows about.
   - Skill discovery from a fresh shell finds the skill via the agent-tier path.

2. Without `--global`, `ddx install` writes to ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/ and links into <project>/.claude/skills/<name> AND <project>/.agents/skills/<name>. In convention mode (no <project>/.ddx/) the project's agent-tier paths still point into the XDG project dir's plugins. In in-tree mode they point into <project>/.ddx/plugins/.

3. `ddx init --global` is the umbrella command for first-time setup: installs the default ddx plugin globally, creates agent-tier links, and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults.

4. `ddx init` (no flag) creates <project>/.ddx/ and does NOT install plugins — plugins are lazy-resolved on first use, preferring the project install over global.

5. Plugin lookup precedence at runtime:
   - <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/ (or ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/ in convention mode) if present.
   - ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ otherwise.
   - Hardcoded baked-in fallback (the default ddx plugin only).

6. The "ddx-validate" pre-commit hook (per ddx-777f4a09 already landed) must continue to scope its checks correctly: skill-related validation runs only when skill files are staged, regardless of install location.

7. `ddx doctor` reports both install layers: "Global Install (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx) — ok | missing" and "Project Install (ddxroot.Path()/plugins/ddx) — ok | missing | lazy-resolves-to-global".

SPEC IMPACT (in addition to ddx-06cbaa90's spec updates):
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-025-or-equivalent (skill install topology) — file or update.
- library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md "How this skill works" section — document install locations.
- library/skills/ddx/reference/agents.md (if exists) — document agent-tier paths.
- CLAUDE.md "Persona System" section — document install precedence.

NON-SCOPE
- Removing the existing per-project install entirely (both modes coexist).
- Migrating ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths if any exist (likely none today).
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex (add others as those harnesses define their skill paths).

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — install paths route through ddxroot.Path() AND the new ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ path. Both must be agreed-on before this .ddx/beads.jsonl:336:{"acceptance":"1. TestPluginLookup_PrefersProjectOverGlobal (cli/internal/registry/resolve_test.go) asserts that when both the project plugins/<name> dir and ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name> exist, ResolvePlugin returns the project path with layer=="project".
2. TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal (cli/internal/registry/resolve_test.go) asserts that when the project copy is absent but the global copy exists, ResolvePlugin returns the global path with layer=="global".
3. A test asserts ResolvePlugin returns layer=="baked-in" with empty path for name=="ddx" when neither project nor global copy exists, and a non-nil error for any other unresolved name.
4. The skill-install-topology ADR file exists and documents the project→global→baked-in precedence; the "Persona System" precedence text in CLAUDE.md and cli/cmd/CLAUDE.md matches it.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/registry/... green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"9a1fd9da9ca03fdf83540ce34b5890966824d755","created_at":"2026-06-03T12:37:10.32456447Z","description":"PROBLEM (epic ddx-602ae31a, Principle 2 — "Platform Services in CLI, Opinions in Workflows")

Skills/personas/plugins can live in two install layers (project-local and machine-global). Runtime resolution must deterministically prefer the project copy, fall back to the global copy, and only then use the baked-in default ddx plugin. This resolution function is the foundation every install/update/doctor surface depends on, so it is split out first.

ROOT CAUSE
- The resolution helper lives at cli/internal/registry/resolve.go:29 `ResolvePlugin(ctx, projectRoot, name)`. It must return path + layer ("project"|"global"|"baked-in") with the documented precedence and a non-nil error for any other unresolved name. The project path is computed via `ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)` (resolve.go:30) which already handles in-tree vs convention mode; the global path via `ddxroot.GlobalDir()` (resolve.go:35). Baked-in is valid only for name=="ddx" (resolve.go:40).
- The governing decision (skill install topology) and the precedence documentation in CLAUDE.md must match this behavior.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Ensure cli/internal/registry/resolve.go:29 `ResolvePlugin` implements precedence project → global → baked-in exactly, returning empty path for the baked-in layer (callers use defaultplugin.FS()).
2. Land/refresh the skill-install-topology ADR (docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-skill-install-topology.md or the equivalent ADR referenced by CLAUDE.md) describing the three-layer precedence.
3. Confirm the "Persona System" sections of CLAUDE.md and cli/cmd/CLAUDE.md document the precedence (project-local → ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx → baked-in default).

NON-SCOPE
- The `--global` install/update CLI surface (sibling child).
- init/doctor wiring (sibling child).
- Removing the existing per-project install entirely (both modes coexist).
- Migrating any ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — `ResolvePlugin` routes through `ddxroot.Path()` and `ddxroot.GlobalDir()`; both must be agreed-on before this lands.","events_attachment":"ddx-9ae1192f/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-9ae1192f","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:plugin","area:config","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-c6148486","status":"closed","title":"registry: plugin lookup precedence project→global→baked-in + skill-install-topology spec","updated_at":"2026-06-03T12:54:00.440122017Z"}

.ddx/attachments/ddx-6ff597ca/events.jsonl:14:{"actor":"ddx","body":"post-merge review: REQUEST_CHANGES
**AC 7 — command path**: The command is registered as `ddx doc audit` (`cli/cmd/doc.go:59`), but the acceptance criteria specifies `ddx docs audit` (or `ddx doctor docs`). If `doc` is the existing noun, this is arguably fine, but the AC should be reconciled. The `Aliases: []string{"integrity"}` alias doesn't cover the spec'd names.
**AC 7 — `--json` exit code**: `cli/cmd/doc.go:79-83` — when `--json` is set, the command encodes issues and returns `nil` unconditionally. The AC requires exit 1 when issues are present regardless of output format, so CI pipelines using `ddx doc audit --json` would incorrectly report success on a broken graph. The test `TestDocAuditCommand_JSONOutput` even documents this as intentional ("--json always succeeds (exit 0) so piping into jq works"), which contradicts the AC. Either the AC should be amended or the `--json` path should also return `ExitError` when issues exist (with a `--exit-zero` escape hatch for pipe-friendly use).

Steps:
1. Add Makefile target copy-skills in cli/Makefile that rsyncs skills/ddx/ into cli/internal/skills/ddx/ before every build. Example:
     copy-skills:
         rsync -a --delete ../skills/ddx/ ./internal/skills/ddx/
2. Wire copy-skills as a prerequisite of make build, make test, make dev (and any other targets that invoke go build or go test).
3. Update cli/internal/skills/embed.go to replace the current multi-dir embed line with: //go:embed all:ddx
4. Delete cli/internal/skills/ddx-{bead,agent,doctor,install,status,review,run}/ — the new embed picks up the copied ddx/ tree instead. (Deletion of top-level /skills/ddx-* is Phase 2; this bead only touches cli/internal/skills/.)
5. Commit cli/internal/skills/ddx/ as a tracked copy so contributors who run go build directly (without make) still get a valid binary. Add a pre-commit hook (lefthook.yml) that re-runs make copy-skills if skills/ddx/ is dirty and cli/internal/skills/ddx/ is stale; this keeps the copy in sync without manual intervention.

In-scope files:
- cli/Makefile
- cli/internal/skills/embed.go
- cli/internal/skills/ddx/ (committed copy — gets populated by copy-skills)
- lefthook.yml (add pre-commit hook)

Out-of-scope:
- skills/ddx/SKILL.md and reference files — separate beads
- ddx init / ddx update changes — separate beads
- Old /skills/ddx-* (top-level) deletion — Phase 2

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:121:{"acceptance":"1. `TestExecuteBeadRejectsCommitPlusNoChangesRationale` or equivalent proves an attempt with a synthesized implementation commit and `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/no_changes_rationale.txt` does not return `status=success`, `outcome=merged`, or close the bead.
2. `TestExecuteBeadMixedCommitAndBlockedNoChangesRationalePreservesEvidence` or equivalent proves a mixed result containing `status: blocked` preserves the rationale text in result/evidence and exposes a clear `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale`-style detail.
3. Existing legitimate zero-diff no_changes coverage still passes, including `TestNoChangesRationaleWithVerificationCommandClosesAlreadySatisfied` or the local equivalent for verification_command behavior.
4. Existing normal commit success coverage still passes when no `no_changes_rationale.txt` is present.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestExecuteBead.*NoChanges|TestNoChanges.*|Test.*Mixed.*NoChanges" -count=1` passes.
6. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/...` passes or any pre-existing unrelated failure is recorded in the bead evidence without being converted to success.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","archived_at":"2026-05-15T00:08:56Z","closing_commit_sha":"694426cc7e302ed91531fbf6a082c6b7c5a0d21c","created_at":"2026-05-12T05:23:44.206444045Z","description":"PROBLEM
A DDx execute-bead attempt can produce both a substantive implementation commit and `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/no_changes_rationale.txt`, and DDx still records the attempt as `success`/`merged`. Live evidence: attempt `20260512T050728-bb18a61f` for `ddx-ef9e8aec` landed commit `15772fc85`, merged evidence commit `9e1ab8506`, and closed the bead, while `.ddx/executions/20260512T050728-bb18a61f/no_changes_rationale.txt` says `status: blocked` because the required `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/...` gate timed out/failed. That turns a self-reported blocked verification into a successful close.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1413-1428` classifies `task_succeeded` solely from `resultRev != baseRev`; it only reads `no_changes_rationale.txt` when `resultRev == baseRev`.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:615-616` deliberately excludes `.ddx/executions/*/no_changes_rationale.txt` from synthesized code commits, so the file can coexist with a synthesized implementation commit without affecting `resultRev`.
- `cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:56-90` already parses structured `status: blocked|open|proposed` no_changes rationales, but that parser is bypassed when any implementation commit exists.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1360-1407` treats the returned report as authoritative; by the time the loop sees the result, the contradictory no_changes signal has been lost behind a success/merged outcome.

PROPOSED FIX
- Make execute-bead treat an implementation diff plus `no_changes_rationale.txt` as a non-successful mixed-signal result before close/merge success is reported. A worker must choose one lane: commit green implementation, or no_changes/status rationale.
- Preserve the rationale text in evidence and surface a clear failure/detail such as `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale` so the worker can retry or a follow-up bead can handle the stated blocker.
- Ensure `status: blocked` in the rationale cannot close a bead as success when a commit also exists.
- Keep legitimate `no_changes` behavior unchanged for zero-diff attempts and keep ordinary green implementation commits unchanged when no rationale file exists.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_*test.go` or `cli/internal/agent/try/*test.go` as needed for focused coverage

NON-SCOPE
- Do not rewrite the already-landed `ddx-ef9e8aec` history.
- Do not change Fizeau routing/model/provider selection.
- Do not weaken legitimate already-satisfied no_changes verification.

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:184:{"acceptance":"1. `TestPostAttemptTriageHook_MissingSkillErrorIncludesProjectRootPathsAndRemediation` verifies the missing-skill error includes the resolved project root, both checked nested skill paths, `ddx update --force`, and `ddx doctor`.
2. `TestRunPostAttemptTriage_MissingSkillStillFailOpen` verifies a missing lifecycle skill logs the improved diagnostic but returns the original `ExecuteBeadReport` unchanged.
3. `TestHasBeadLifecycleSkillDiagnostics_FindsAgentsOrClaudePath` verifies the diagnostic helper reports success when either `.agents/.../bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` or `.claude/.../bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` exists.
4. `TestPreDispatchLintHook_MissingSkillUsesSameDiagnostic` verifies lint/readiness missing-skill errors use the same checked-path/remediation wording or shared error type.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'Test(PostAttemptTriageHook_MissingSkill|RunPostAttemptTriage_MissingSkill|HasBeadLifecycleSkillDiagnostics|PreDispatchLintHook_MissingSkill)'` green.
6. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/...` green.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","archived_at":"2026-05-15T00:08:56Z","closing_commit_sha":"8a181b4c942f8d4a3b82144be191599c467ed1e6","created_at":"2026-05-06T22:45:03.194529263Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-1c3a78f2","depends_on_id":"ddx-11f0cbf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-07T22:55:43Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
When the post-attempt triage hook cannot find the nested `bead-lifecycle` skill, the operator sees `post-attempt triage error (<bead> execution_failed): triage hook: skill missing: bead-lifecycle (continuing)`. The message is technically true but does not say which project root was inspected, which paths were checked, or what command remediates the install.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go:90-92` returns `triage hook: skill missing: bead-lifecycle` when `hasBeadLifecycleSkill(projectRoot)` is false. `cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go:218-227` hard-codes the checked paths but returns only a boolean, so callers cannot include the checked path list in diagnostics. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:175-179` logs the hook error verbatim and appends `(continuing)`, which confirms fail-open behavior but hides the actionable remediation.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace the boolean-only missing-skill path with a diagnostic helper that returns whether the lifecycle skill exists, the resolved project root, and the exact checked paths.
- Update post-attempt triage errors to say the hook was skipped/degraded because `bead-lifecycle` was missing under the project root.
- Include the checked paths `.agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` and `.claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` in the error text.
- Include remediation in the hook-time error: run `ddx update --force`, then `ddx doctor`.
- Preserve fail-open behavior: missing lifecycle skill must not change the underlying attempt status or close/park behavior by itself.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not run full `ddx doctor` from inside the hook.
- Do not dispatch the lifecycle hook through a fallback global skill path.
- Do not change lint/readiness/triage JSON output contracts.
- Do not change retry or cooldown policy in this bead.

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:191:{"acceptance":"1. On a host where ddx agent check reports at least one LM Studio provider
   (bragi, grendel, vidar, vidar-coder) connected, ddx agent work
   --once --json against a ready bead routes a cheap-tier attempt to a live
   provider without requiring --provider or --harness on the CLI.
2. The result.json for the cheap-tier attempt records status != "execution_failed"
   when the live provider accepts the request (status may be success, no_changes,
   or a non-dispatch failure downstream, but must NOT be "did not match any
   provider, catalog ref, or model-route" and must NOT be "harness lmstudio
   not available: not found in PATH").
3. New unit / integration test in the execute-bead routing package asserts:
   given a catalog entry cheap.embedded-openai: qwen3.5-27b and a discovered
   provider advertising qwen3.5-27b, the resolver returns that provider.
4. New unit / integration test asserts: the lmstudio harness is treated as
   HTTP-probeable, not PATH-probeable, so it survives harness availability
   checks when the live-discovery path sees an LM Studio provider.
5. cd cli && go test ./... && go build .
6. ddx agent doctor and ddx agent check exit 0 on a host with live LM Studio
   providers.","archived_at":"2026-05-07T04:29:14Z","created_at":"2026-04-17T02:49:32.207204061Z","description":"Observed on 2026-04-17T02:39 during an work --once cycle against bead
helix-ede8085a (from /Users/erik/Projects/helix). Evidence preserved at
/Users/erik/Projects/helix/.ddx/executions/20260417T023917-dc4a49d3/result.json
and /Users/erik/Projects/helix/.ddx/executions/20260417T023921-e66e9375/result.json.

ddx agent check --verbose on this host reports live LM Studio providers:
bragi (10 models), grendel (3), vidar (11), vidar-coder (11), openrouter (345).
So model discovery IS working — the gap is in execute-bead's catalog→provider
resolution.

Symptom 1 — cheap tier / embedded-openai surface
  Routing decision: tier=cheap harness=agent model=qwen3.5-27b
  Failure: "model \"qwen3.5-27b\" did not match any provider, catalog ref, or
  model-route; specify --provider or --model-ref explicitly"
  The user's ~/.ddx/model-catalog.yaml maps cheap.embedded-openai → qwen3.5-27b,
  but the embedded ddx-agent harness has no provider registration that serves
  qwen3.5-27b. Meanwhile bragi / grendel / vidar all expose lmstudio-backed
  models and could plausibly serve it — but execute-bead never consults live
  provider discovery when a catalog model-ref fails to resolve.

Symptom 2 — lmstudio harness fallback
  Routing decision: harness=lmstudio model=minimax/minimax-m2.7
  Failure: "agent: harness lmstudio not available:  not found in PATH"
  lmstudio is an HTTP harness (ddx agent doctor reports it as "(http)" and OK),
  yet the execute-bead harness probe path-checks it like a binary CLI and
  rejects it before any HTTP probe runs.

Net effect: the auto-escalation ladder skips cheap and standard tiers entirely
on this machine and lands on smart-tier codex/gpt-5.4, defeating the
cost-tiered routing the parent epic (ddx-4b59b816) is designed to enable.

The drain loop still treats no-progress mostly as base_rev == result_rev after a failed implementation attempt. That is too broad now that ddx work has pre-claim intake, pre-dispatch lint, routing preflight, review malfunctions, operator parks, and other non-implementation outcomes. Those states should not consume no-progress retry budget or trigger long cooldowns.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1964-1978 implements shouldSuppressNoProgress with only three checks: disrupted, transient outcome reason, and base/result equality.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1980-1987 only treats transport, quota, routing, timeout, merge_conflict, lock contention, and no viable provider as transient; it omits intake blocks, decomposition, claim races, routing preflight, auth/tool setup, review errors, and needs_human/operator states.
- cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:230-249 increments no_changes metadata before the work layer has a reusable valid-implementation-attempt classifier.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1199-1215 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1262-1275 apply no-progress cooldowns from separate branches instead of a shared eligibility decision.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a reusable no-progress eligibility helper, either in cli/internal/agent/try or cli/internal/agent/work, that answers whether the implementer had a real opportunity to inspect/modify/verify code.
- Count no-progress only for valid implementation attempts with checkout/context established and an implementer result available.
- Exclude intake rewrites/blocks/decomposition, claim races, routing preflight, quota, transport, auth/tool setup, review errors, needs_human/operator-required states, and other system failures.
- Route eligible no-progress stops through the StopCondition NoProgress vocabulary from ddx-89ab3fda.
- Preserve existing behavior for genuine no-commit implementation attempts.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change review verdict taxonomy beyond marking review/system outcomes no-progress-ineligible.
- Do not schedule repair cycles.
- Do not alter max-cost handling.

DEPS
Depends on ddx-89ab3fda for the NoProgress StopCondition vocabulary.","events_attachment":"ddx-224ffcd2/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-224ffcd2","issue_type":"task","labels":["adr:024","area:agent","area:try","area:work","kind:fix","phase:2","reliability","spec:FEAT-010","split-from:ddx-9228a484","story:10"],"parent":"ddx-5cb6e6cd","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-010","split-from":"ddx-9228a484","status":"closed","title":"try: count no-progress only for valid implementation attempts","updated_at":"2026-05-07T18:31:57.184074831Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:214:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As an operator, I want a single view that shows every place my agents can run — endpoint providers and subprocess harnesses alike — with status, recent token usage, and quota utilization. When I click into one, I see a 7-day trend so I can tell if I'm about to run out.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. **Deliverable 1 — Unified view.**
   - New `harnessStatuses` GraphQL resolver returns subprocess harnesses (claude, codex, gemini, agent) with: name, kind=`harness`, reachable, detail, lastCheckedAt, defaultForProfile.
   - Existing `providerStatuses` shape gains `kind=\"endpoint\"` and `lastCheckedAt`.
   - UI page loads within 500ms on a warm cache — it renders from last-known status **first**, then patches rows live as async probes return. Playwright asserts the table is interactive within 500ms of navigation and that row status patches within 5s of the probe completing.
   - Subprocess harnesses claude and codex **appear in the table** on a machine where their binaries are installed, with correct reachable status. Integration test.

2. **Deliverable 2 — Usage + quota fields (stubbable).**
   - Per-row fields `usage` and `quota` as described. All fields nullable.
   - For endpoint providers, `usage.tokensUsedLast24h` and `usage.tokensUsedLastHour` are computed from the sessions index (fixture: ≥1k session rows covering the last 24h). Unit test asserts correctness on a seeded window.
   - For subprocess harnesses that expose rate-limit headers (claude, codex), parsed headers populate `quota.ceilingTokens`, `quota.ceilingWindowSeconds`, `quota.remaining`, `quota.resetAt`. Parser tested with captured header fixtures from each harness.
   - For harnesses/providers that expose nothing, fields remain null and the UI renders `—` / "not reported"; no fabricated data.
   - Utilization bar renders when both `usage` and `quota.ceilingTokens` are present. Sparkline renders when ≥6 hourly buckets of usage are available.

3. **Deliverable 3 — Trend detail.**
   - Detail route `/nodes/.../providers/[name]` shows 7-day and 30-day tokens/hour + requests/hour time series, with quota ceiling overlay when known.
   - Buckets computed server-side from the sessions index; the client receives ≤ 200 data points per series.
   - "Projected to hit quota in ~X" callout uses last-24h slope only. Renders only when `quota.ceilingTokens` is known and the last-24h slope is non-zero positive; otherwise absent.
   - Playwright asserts the detail route renders with seeded usage and the projection text appears for a fixture that has a ceiling + ascending usage.

4. **Perf posture.**
   - Fixture for perf test: ≥1,000 session index rows covering the last 30 days with usage distributed across ≥5 endpoints/harnesses. Per standing directive in `ddx-9ce6842a`.
   - Targets: unified list p95 ≤ 200ms HTTP (excluding async probes); trend detail p95 ≤ 400ms HTTP on the fixture.
   - If in-place aggregation cannot hit the detail-view p95 on a 10,000-row stretch fixture, pause and raise the DB-substrate decision with the user per the standing directive — do not silently switch.

5. **Design note.**
   - One-pager in `docs/helix/02-design/` before implementation recording the page-rename decision, the row model (common fields, kind-specific fields), async-probe strategy, and the quota source per harness/provider. PR links to it.

6. **No regressions.**
   - `ddx agent providers`, `ddx agent doctor`, `ddx agent list`, `ddx agent check` CLI commands continue to work unchanged.
   - `defaultRouteStatus` query remains.

7. **Cross-references.**
   - Depends on `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions index) for usage aggregation. If that bead is not yet landed when this work starts, ship Deliverable 1 standalone with usage/quota stubbed as "not available," and re-open the remaining deliverables when the index is ready.","archived_at":"2026-05-07T04:29:14Z","closing_commit_sha":"7ef7a4b1c517483485821f99aec728292bbe83a1","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:48:37.871527387Z","description":"## Observed

Three related issues on `/nodes/.../providers`:

1. **Loads slowly.** The page fires two GraphQL queries on mount — `providerStatuses` (which probes every configured provider for live connectivity) and `defaultRouteStatus` (which resolves the default route). Both are synchronous and serialized only by `Promise.all`. Each provider probe is a real network call.
2. **Missing harnesses.** The pane shows API-endpoint providers (qwen, minimax, etc. — anything wired through the service with an endpoint). It does not show subprocess-invoked harnesses like `claude` (Claude Code) or `codex`. That is accurate to the current `providerStatuses` resolver (`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_providers.go:15-50`, which calls `svc.ListProviders`) but not what an operator wants: the operator thinks of "where their agent work can run," which includes both endpoint providers AND subprocess harnesses.
3. **No quota visibility.** Operator need: see current token utilization rate + quota utilization per harness/provider, trend it, and use the trend to decide whether to throttle before hitting a cap.

## Root cause by area

- **Slow load:** `svc.ListProviders` probes every configured provider for liveness. Probes are serial within the service call, and the whole query blocks page render.
- **Missing harnesses:** Subprocess harnesses (claude, codex, gemini) are first-class in the CLI (`ddx agent list`, `ddx agent doctor`, `ddx agent check`) but are not surfaced via `providerStatuses`. They have their own status concepts — binary installed, credentials configured, rate-limit headroom — that don't map cleanly onto the endpoint-provider model.
- **No quota:** No existing data source captures rolling token usage vs quota. Quota information varies wildly by harness: Claude Code has message / session headroom concepts from response headers; codex has rate-limit headers; endpoint providers have API quota per key. There is no unified representation today.

## Proposed direction

Three deliverables, sequenced from easy to hard.

### Deliverable 1 — Unified "Agent endpoints" view (renames/rescopes the page)

- Rename the surface from "Providers" to **"Agent endpoints"** (or similar — confirm copy in the design note). The domain object is "a place where agent work can run," not "an API provider."
- Merge the data: `providerStatuses` stays as the source for endpoint providers. A new `harnessStatuses` resolver (read from `svc.ListHarnesses` / existing `ddx agent list` + `ddx agent doctor` machinery) surfaces subprocess harnesses. Page renders both in one table with a `kind` column (`endpoint` | `harness`) — or two tables if design prefers.
- Status fields common to both: name, kind, reachable (bool), detail (text), last-checked-at, default-for-profile (set of profile names).
- Asynchronous liveness. The page renders an instant first paint from cached status; liveness probes fire in background and patch rows as they return. No synchronous probe-wall on load.

### Deliverable 2 — Token & quota fields

- Extend the status payload (per row) with: `usage { tokensUsedLastHour, tokensUsedLast24h, requestsLastHour, requestsLast24h }` and `quota { ceilingTokens?, ceilingWindowSeconds?, remaining?, resetAt? }`. Any field may be null if the provider/harness doesn't expose it.
- Data sources:
  - **Endpoint providers:** aggregate from the sessions index (`ddx-2ceb02fa`) filtered to the last 1h / 24h, grouped by provider. Quota ceilings come from any headers we capture during calls, plus optional static config.
  - **Subprocess harnesses:** same sessions-index aggregation for usage. Quota — parse rate-limit headers from the harness output where available (Claude Code, codex both emit them). For harnesses that don't, leave quota null and render "not reported."
- Row gains two small inline displays: utilization bar (used / ceiling for the quota window) and a 7-day sparkline of tokens/hour.

### Deliverable 3 — Trend view

- Click into a row → detail page showing: 7-day and 30-day time-series of tokens/hour and requests/hour, with the quota ceiling overlaid. Aggregations come from the sessions index, bucketed server-side to avoid shipping raw rows.
- Simple "projected to hit quota in ~X hours/days" callout computed from the slope of the last 24h — enough to answer "are we on a trend to run out?" without building a forecasting system.

## Cross-bead dependencies

- Depends on `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions index) for any usage-rate data. If that bead isn't landed when this work starts, the usage/quota features stub to "not available" and ship purely with the unified-view rename + async liveness.
- Fixture / perf guidance: per the standing directive recorded in `ddx-9ce6842a`, any aggregation here is tested against ≥1k session records. If in-place aggregation can't hit perf targets, bring the DB-substrate question to the user before picking one.","events_attachment":"ddx-23978824/events.jsonl","feature":"FEAT-008","id":"ddx-23978824","issue_type":"task","labels":["feat-008","feat-006","feat-014","providers","observability"],"notes":"Cross-reference: ddx-6904a90b splits session costs into paid, subscription-equivalent, and local billingMode buckets; the unified endpoints usage/quota work should reuse those billingMode semantics when distinguishing paid endpoints, subscription harnesses, and local endpoints.

When a pre-land reviewer finds a fixable implementation or test gap, DDx should repair in the same still-live attempt worktree instead of landing a rejected candidate or starting from a cold worktree. No same-worktree repair loop exists today.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:233-244 records REQUEST_CHANGES as a non-approved review outcome but does not launch an implementer repair pass.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1481-1487 includes a review gate prompt block, but the normal implementation prompt also says commit exactly once at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1520-1523 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1550-1555; repair needs a distinct prompt mode that permits one append-only repair commit.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1086-1123 counts review outcomes but has no loop that appends repair commits and re-runs checks/review before final land.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a RepairPass that runs in the same attempt worktree when ReviewFindingClassifier returns review_fixable_gap.
- Build a repair prompt that includes prior review findings, per-AC failures, failed candidate_rev, base_rev, required verification commands, and explicit append-only commit rules.
- Permit one repair commit per repair cycle while forbidding reset, amend, squash, rebase, or history rewrite.
- After each repair commit, pin a new candidate ref, re-run checks, re-run review against base_rev..new_candidate_rev, and stop on approval, manual class, repair_max_cycles, no-progress, provider/cost budget, or context cancellation.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not enable two-slot review; this uses the transitional single-slot review path.
- Do not implement concrete model fallback; routing power floors are a separate bead.

DEPS
Depends on classification so only review_fixable_gap enters repair.","id":"ddx-2471b63d","issue_type":"task","labels":["adr:024","area:agent","area:review","candidate-cycle","kind:feature","phase:2","reliability","repair","spec:FEAT-010"],"owner":"erik","parent":"ddx-31e07fdc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"closed","title":"repair: run same-worktree append-only repair cycles","updated_at":"2026-05-09T04:43:25.350373076Z","work-heartbeat-at":"2026-05-09T04:35:37.478860602Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:232:{"acceptance":"1. TestWorkInterrupt_InFlightAttemptUnclaimsBead verifies cancelling the work command context during an in-flight attempt releases the claim and leaves the bead open/re-claimable.
2. TestWorkInterrupt_DoesNotSetNoProgressCooldown verifies interrupted work does not set `work-retry-after` or increment no-progress suppression.
3. TestWorkInterrupt_RemovesClaimLiveness verifies external claim heartbeat/liveness is removed or stale-safe after cleanup.
4. TestTryInterrupt_InFlightAttemptUnclaimsTarget verifies `ddx try <id>` cancellation leaves the target bead re-claimable and exits non-zero with an interrupted/signal-class outcome.
5. TestTryInterrupt_DoesNotReportOperatorCancel verifies plain context cancellation is not classified as `operator_cancel` unless the bead cancel marker was present.
6. TestInterruptedAfterTerminalMutation_DoesNotUndoClose verifies cancellation after a successful terminal mutation does not reopen or unclaim a closed bead.
7. `rg -n "context.Canceled|context_canceled|signal|operator_cancel|work-retry-after" cli/internal/agent cli/cmd` shows cancellation paths are explicit and comments distinguish signal interruption from operator cancel.
8. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... -run "TestWorkInterrupt|TestTryInterrupt|TestInterrupted|TestCancel|TestClaim" -count=1` passes.
9. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","archived_at":"2026-05-07T04:29:14Z","closing_commit_sha":"278562820307c6dc13cbe116ebcd9bc931cd56b3","created_at":"2026-05-06T15:17:45.453929652Z","description":"PROBLEM
After the root context is cancelled by Ctrl-C/SIGTERM, `ddx work` and `ddx try` must finish the TD-031 interruption path: preserve available evidence, classify the attempt as mechanically disrupted, release any non-terminal bead claim, remove runtime liveness state, and leave the unfinished bead re-claimable. The current code has partial context-cancellation classification but needs explicit end-to-end coverage for the CLI work/try paths.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:859-878 marks non-success reports as disrupted when ctx is cancelled, but the surrounding cleanup/unclaim behavior is only incidentally covered by generic failure branches.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:812-846 starts and stops claim heartbeat around an attempt; interrupted attempts must stop liveness and release or clear claims deterministically.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:797-807 creates a dispatch context and only the bead-extra cancel path forces `operator_cancel`; plain context cancellation should remain `signal`/mechanical disruption, not `operator_cancel`.
- cli/cmd/try.go:333-342 and cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1921-1940 propagate `cmd.Context()` into worker.Run but need regression tests that cancellation leaves the target bead re-claimable and reports the signal path.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:289-314 defines the claim release and no-cooldown requirements for worker shutdown/interruption.

PROPOSED FIX
- Make interrupted attempts produce a stable disrupted/interrupted report reason (for example `context_canceled` / `signal`) without counting as model no-progress.
- Ensure the loop unclaims interrupted non-terminal beads and removes any external claim heartbeat/liveness record.
- Ensure interrupted attempts do not set `work-retry-after` unless a separate retryable condition was already recorded before cancellation.
- Ensure `ddx try <id>` reports a clear interrupted outcome and exits non-zero after cleanup.
- Add regression tests around worker.Run plus command-level try/work cancellation paths using controllable fake executors/runners.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not preserve worktrees as resumable by default. Cleanup/retry-from-scratch is the reliable default.

Operators must manually recover by checking out the dangling SHA, verifying ACs, and merging.

REPRO (observed in axon project, 2026-05-08):
- Bead axon-d323acc9 ("refactor(axon-schema): import named-query parser from axon-cypher-ast")
- Attempt 20260508T190516-8d61a244, session eb-b068c4db
- result.json fields: outcome=task_succeeded, status=success, result_rev=c3386193ab1c18fdf5739901c41d2e69093f1030, base_rev=9194da47097b2d0d05899a51688950084b3995b8, duration_ms=1179708, exit_code=0
- Master HEAD never advanced to include c3386193; bead remained in_progress until manual recovery
- A subsequent attempt 20260508T192511-c4a200de fired 15 seconds later and recorded outcome=task_no_evidence with detail "agent exited without a commit or no_changes_rationale.txt", which suggests the worker re-ran and saw no work to do (the success was already in the dangling commit) but did not connect that to the prior success record.

- Both attempt result.json files are present and parsable.
- Recovery required: git merge --no-ff <result_rev> + ddx bead close --commit <result_rev> --session <session_id>.
- No evidence in the bead events indicated the attempt had succeeded; the success was discovered by inspecting result.json on disk.

PROPOSED FIX DIRECTION (owned by ddx-loop/finalize maintainer):
1. The post-execute finalize step (merge + bead update with success event) should be transactional with attempt outcome, or at minimum idempotent so a follow-up ddx work cycle can complete it.
2. On bead pick, if status=in_progress and the most-recent attempt outcome=success but no merge commit references the result_rev, attempt to land that result before claiming new work or starting a fresh attempt.
3. A startup self-check could enumerate result.json files where outcome=task_succeeded and ensure the corresponding result_rev is reachable from a branch; surface inconsistencies as recoverable state.

ddx work currently continues scanning and claiming ready beads after setup/infrastructure failures. During the May 6 ENOSPC incident, ddx work failed to create one isolated worktree, then immediately claimed another production-reachability bead and failed again. Resource exhaustion must stop the drain because continuing expands the blast radius.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:437-530 initializes loop events and exitReason but does not include resource_exhausted as a recognized loop-fatal exit reason.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:529 onward continues iterating after non-success reports unless existing retry/no-progress/once logic stops it; there is no host-resource circuit breaker.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go:263-283 defines status taxonomy around execution_failed/no_evidence/etc., but there is no distinct resource_exhausted status/reason for loop policy to inspect.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:434-440 and docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:281-284 require ddx work to stop claiming new beads after resource_exhausted.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a stable resource_exhausted attempt/loop classification that can be returned by ddx try/resource preflight and recognized by ExecuteBeadWorker.
- On resource_exhausted, release any non-terminal claim, emit cleanup/resource events, set loop.end exit_reason=resource_exhausted, and return without scanning the next ready bead.
- Print an operator-visible message such as resource exhausted after cleanup; stopping work loop.
- Ensure resource_exhausted is not counted as model no-progress, capability retry, or review/quota retry.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement the resource checker itself here; this bead consumes the outcome from the try/resource-preflight bead.
- Do not change queue ranking or production-reachability bead priority.
- Do not change provider 429 in-attempt retry behavior.","events_attachment":"ddx-2d49729e/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-2d49729e","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:cli","cleanup","kind:bug","phase:2","reconciled:no-changes-state","reliability","resource-exhaustion","spec:FEAT-010","spec:SD-025"],"parent":"ddx-f3dfde0d","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-5799042e","spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"closed","title":"work: stop queue drain on resource_exhausted attempts","updated_at":"2026-05-07T05:58:24.734024798Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:278:{"acceptance":"ddx agent work --harness claude --model claude-sonnet-4-6 invokes the claude binary, not ddx-agent; session log shows model: claude-sonnet-4-6 not model: vidar or model: bragi; if --model specifies an unrecognized claude model, the error is surfaced before any bead is claimed; ddx agent work --harness agent --model vidar continues to work as before (regression guard)","archived_at":"2026-05-07T04:29:14Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-14T07:45:16Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1412283","closing_commit_sha":"e0f5fca79640c9ba54c8ef27113597e7e4dfaeaa","created_at":"2026-04-13T20:50:15.374772587Z","description":"When work is invoked with both --harness claude and --model <provider-name> (e.g. --model claude-sonnet-4-6), the --harness flag is silently ignored and the embedded agent harness is used instead, routing to whichever LM Studio provider is configured as default.

Observed: started work --local --harness claude --model claude-sonnet-4-6. Every session in the output showed 'model: vidar' or 'model: bragi' and all LLM responses were from qwen3.5-27b. No claude traffic was generated. The claude binary is healthy (ddx agent doctor reports OK). When work is started with --harness claude and NO --model flag, the claude binary is correctly invoked (confirmed by claude --print process appearing in ps output).

Root cause hypothesis: the --model flag value is being interpreted as a provider/route key by the embedded agent harness dispatch layer. When the model string matches a known provider name or a catalog entry that maps to the embedded-openai surface, the harness selection silently switches to 'agent' regardless of what --harness specifies. The --harness flag loses the race.

Required fix: --harness must take strict precedence over any routing implied by --model. If --harness claude is specified and the model string is not a valid claude model ID, the loop should either (a) pass the model string through to the claude binary as-is and let it fail with a clear error, or (b) emit a startup warning 'model X is not a recognized claude model; ignoring --model flag' and proceed with the harness default. Silent rerouting to a different harness is never acceptable.

Additionally: when the harness override causes a routing mismatch, the error or warning must appear before any bead is claimed, not after execution has started.","events_attachment":"ddx-2ef94940/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-2ef94940","issue_type":"bug","labels":["bug","work","cli","ux"],"owner":"ddx","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-5e97635e","status":"closed","title":"work: --harness flag silently ignored when --model is a provider name","updated_at":"2026-04-14T08:13:37.379499959Z","work-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-14T08:13:16.992411409Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:366:{"acceptance":"- [ ] New command or subcommand: `ddx doctor divergence` or `ddx divergence check` prints the divergence report
- [ ] Thresholds are configurable via .ddx/config.yaml (default: 5 commits each side, 7 days merge-base age)
- [ ] work refuses to start in ERROR state unless --force passed
- [ ] Structured event 'divergence_status' emitted at loop start and on every doctor run
- [ ] Optional webhook integration via config.yaml alert_webhook_url
- [ ] Unit tests: each state (clean, ahead, behind, diverged, network-down, drift)
- [ ] Integration test: run the loop on a diverged repo and observe the refuse-to-start behavior
- [ ] Docs: README section on divergence monitoring, thresholds, and how to recover","archived_at":"2026-05-07T04:29:14Z","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:21:10.525089812Z","description":"Fifth child of ddx-b7ee0d6f. The 14-day silent divergence on axon (eitri local 134 commits ahead of merge-base, sindri/origin 336 commits ahead) was caught only because a human happened to try pushing. A trivial monitor running a few times a day would have flagged the problem at 5 commits, not 470.

## Required behavior

Add a lightweight divergence check that runs either:
- At the top of each work iteration (cheap, ~50ms), OR
- Periodically via a scheduled task (ddx doctor or similar), OR
- Both

The check computes:
- `git fetch origin <branch>` (tolerant of network failure)
- local_ahead = `git rev-list --count origin/<branch>..HEAD`
- origin_ahead = `git rev-list --count HEAD..origin/<branch>`
- merge_base_age = `git log -1 --format=%ar $(git merge-base HEAD origin/<branch>)`

Emit a structured event 'divergence_status' with these fields. If:
- local_ahead > threshold (default 5): warn
- origin_ahead > threshold (default 5): warn
- both > 0: ERROR — diverged, neither side is a strict ancestor
- merge_base_age > threshold (default 7 days): warn (drift)

Integration surface:
- `ddx doctor` prints the divergence report
- `ddx agent work` refuses to start if in the ERROR state (both sides ahead) unless --force is passed
- An optional webhook / notification channel (config: alert_webhook_url) for ops teams

## Why this is P1 not P0

The fetch/push/rebase triad (P0) prevents NEW divergence. This bead catches EXISTING divergence and alerts before it grows. Without the P0 fixes, this alert would fire constantly; with them, it should fire rarely. Still valuable as defense in depth.

## Test cases

- Fresh clone: report is clean (0 ahead / 0 behind)
- Local 3 commits ahead: report shows local_ahead=3, no warning
- Local 6 commits ahead: report shows local_ahead=6, warning emitted
- Both sides ahead: ERROR status, work refuses to start
- Merge base 10 days old with both sides at 0: drift warning, work continues
- Network unreachable: report shows 'unknown', no ERROR, work continues","id":"ddx-42dd982c","issue_type":"task","labels":["work","monitoring","observability"],"parent":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-d16c6e3","status":"closed","title":"work: divergence monitoring and alerting","updated_at":"2026-04-14T15:56:12.030389438Z"}

AC4. `ddx agent doctor` (or `ddx agent catalog show`) emits a warning when a tier's catalog entries reference a model id that has no matching named route AND no provider /v1/models entry across configured providers, so the user discovers the gap before invoking work.

ResolveRoute is then called with model="cheap"/"standard"/"smart" — strings that match no model id and no named route. Every tier resolves to an empty harness/empty model and is recorded as a tier-attempt with body "no viable harness found". The loop exhausts all tiers and reports "work: all tiers exhausted — no viable provider found".

This is happening today on a workstation that has bragi/vidar/hel hosting qwen3.6, plus working claude and codex harnesses. execute-bead succeeds with the same providers when --harness/--model are pinned explicitly, proving the underlying routing is fine. Only the tier→model resolution path is broken.

The model catalog (~/.ddx/model-catalog.yaml or built-in defaults, surfaced via `ddx agent catalog show`) ALREADY contains the tier→surface→model mapping that would make this work. The resolver just doesn't consume it. Surfaces and concrete models are listed per tier (e.g. cheap: embedded-openai→qwen3.5-27b, codex→gpt-5.4-mini, claude→claude-haiku-4-5) — the resolver should walk these surfaces in order, picking the first whose harness is healthy and whose model resolves to a reachable provider via model_routes. Per-project model_overrides should be an OPTIONAL override layered on top of catalog defaults, not the only thing that makes tier resolution work.

Caller-side workaround: every project must add agent.routing.model_overrides to .ddx/config.yaml before work will function. This is silent: there is no diagnostic from `ddx agent doctor`, no warning from `ddx agent catalog show`, and no hint in the "no viable provider" error pointing at the missing config. Users have to read source to understand why a fully-healthy provider/harness fleet produces zero viable tiers.

Repro:
1. Fresh project with .ddx/config.yaml lacking agent.routing.model_overrides
2. Healthy provider config in ~/.config/agent/config.yaml (any qwen/claude/codex)
3. `ddx agent work --once --local --no-adaptive-min-tier`
4. Every tier-attempt event records "no viable harness found"; loop exits with 'all tiers exhausted'

Related symptom — the adaptive min-tier promotion uses cheap-tier success rate to skip cheap, but with the bug above no cheap-tier attempt has ever produced a successful harness invocation, so the trailing window is biased to 0% by design rather than by actual model performance.","events_attachment":"ddx-5538aa5b/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-5538aa5b","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:routing","kind:design-flaw"],"owner":"ddx","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-f61475d0","status":"closed","title":"work: tier resolver should consume catalog instead of requiring per-project model_overrides","updated_at":"2026-04-29T04:41:11.568927737Z","work-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-29T04:40:26.540559641Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:452:{"archived_at":"2026-05-07T04:29:14Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-11T19:53:03Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2860272","created_at":"2026-04-11T19:33:05.943045249Z","description":"ddx agent work fails on eitri: agent harness routes to z-ai/glm-5.1 ignoring local vidar config

ddx agent work consistently fails on the helix machine (eitri) with provider-level errors.
The work correctly selects and claims beads, but the agent harness never runs the work.
CRITICAL: ddx agent run --harness agent works perfectly on the same machine, pointing to a
model-resolution bug specifically in execute-bead/work.

PROOF: direct agent run works, work fails:
  ddx agent run --text "Hello" --harness agent --timeout 60s → succeeds with qwen3.5-27b on vidar
  ddx agent work --once --json --harness agent → fails with z-ai/glm-5.1 via OpenRouter 404

ROOT CAUSE: Model resolution in execute-bead injects --model z-ai/glm-5.1, overriding the
native ~/.config/agent/config.yaml provider config (default_provider: vidar, model: qwen3.5-27b).
The z-ai/glm-5.1 model is not defined in models.yaml or the native config — it appears to come
from stale execute-bead routing metadata. Even explicit --model flags on work don't
propagate correctly to the spawned execute-bead subprocess.

PROVIDER STATUS on eitri:
- vidar (http://vidar:1234/v1, qwen3.5-27b): WORKS — confirmed with ddx agent run
- bragi (http://bragi:1234/v1, qwen3.5-27b): DOWN — connection refused
- grendel (http://grendel:1234/v1, qwen3.5-27b): DOWN — connection refused
- openrouter (https://openrouter.ai/api/v1): FAILS — 404 guardrail restriction on claude-haiku

MACHINE CONTEXT (eitri, Linux):
- Hostname: eitri, paths use /home/erik/ and linuxbrew
- ~/.config/agent/config.yaml defines providers with default_provider: vidar
- models.yaml defines code-high profile → agent.openai: gpt-5.4 (OpenRouter surface)
- No ~/.ddx.yml config file
- No project-level .agent/config.yaml
- Codex and claude harnesses nearly out of quota — reserved for review, not bulk execution
- work was working previously (existing ddx beads show successful runs)
- z-ai/glm-5.1 string appears nowhere in source — comes from runtime routing metadata

ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES: No AGENT_PROVIDER, AGENT_BASE_URL, AGENT_API_KEY, or AGENT_MODEL set.
All routing comes from native ~/.config/agent/config.yaml.

IMPACT: ddx agent work is the primary execution surface for HELIX beads. If the only
working provider (vidar → qwen3.5-27b) cannot be used from work, entire queues drain
as failures. The loop records each with retry_after (6 hours) and retries indefinitely.

SUGGESTED FIXES:
1. Fix execute-bead model resolution: respect native .agent/config.yaml provider config or
   properly propagate the --model flag from work → execute-bead
2. Add provider health checks: ddx agent doctor should test the actual provider with a
   minimal API call, not just check binary existence
3. Fail open on provider errors: when the default provider fails, fall back to the next
   available provider in config rather than hard-failing
4. Guardrail error clarity: surface OpenRouter 404 as "provider unavailable due to
   account restrictions" rather than generic API error
5. Execute-loop resilience: when all providers fail, mark bead truly blocked instead of
   infinite retry_after cycles

- cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop.go (work model flag propagation)
- cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go (execute-bead model override logic)

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:466:{"acceptance":"1. TD-031 states that DDx startup refuses normal operation when the active bead queue contains legacy lifecycle labels or pseudo-statuses that have not been migrated.
2. FEAT-004 and FEAT-010 state that `ddx work`, server workers, GraphQL worker starts, REST/MCP worker starts, and queue status/readiness commands fail closed on unmigrated lifecycle queues.
3. The docs define the allowed bypass commands: help, version, doctor/read-only diagnostics that do not mutate lifecycle, and `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --apply` / dry-run.
4. The docs explicitly say migration is one-way and git rollback is the recovery strategy; normal runtime does not maintain needs_human or triage:needs-investigation compatibility lanes.
5. The docs require startup error output to include counts of legacy labels/pseudo-statuses and the exact migration command to run.
6. `rg -n "one-way|startup|refuse|migrate --lifecycle|git rollback|needs_human|triage:needs-investigation" docs/helix docs/triage` finds the new migration-gate contract text and no active legacy queue-state wording.
7. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/... ./internal/server/... -run 'TestLifecycle|TestBeadMigrate|Test.*Startup|Test.*Gate'` passes as a regression gate.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","archived_at":"2026-05-15T00:08:56Z","claimed-at":"2026-05-09T06:00:39Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3881837","created_at":"2026-05-08T19:16:04.53061625Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-5995f3bc","depends_on_id":"ddx-da412b9a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-08T19:16:04Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
The lifecycle contract must specify how existing repositories move from label-owned lifecycle state to status-owned lifecycle state. Without a hard startup gate, DDx can start against an old queue, reinterpret stale needs_human / triage:needs-investigation labels under new code, and either run beads that were parked under old semantics or keep old queue lanes alive indefinitely.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/command_factory.go:110-120 constructs the root command without any project-local bead queue format preflight.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:67-92 chooses store backend/prefix but does not identify legacy lifecycle queue state before callers read or mutate beads.
- cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go:10-108 currently defines `ddx bead migrate` only for event externalization/archive and axon backend migration.
- cli/internal/bead/migrate.go:23-40 implements archive/event migration, not lifecycle status migration.
- The live tracker currently contains open beads with needs_human and triage:needs-investigation labels, so this is not a hypothetical upgrade path.

PROPOSED FIX
Extend the governing docs with a one-way lifecycle migration contract. DDx startup must detect unmigrated lifecycle queues and refuse to run normal commands until migration is applied. The allowed escape hatches are only diagnostic/help/version commands and the lifecycle migration command itself. The migration is one-way: old labels and pseudo-statuses are input to the migrator, not a compatibility surface for normal runtime. Because beads are git-tracked, rollback is git rollback rather than maintaining dual semantics.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement the startup gate or migrator in this docs bead. Do not preserve needs_human or triage:needs-investigation as active runtime compatibility aliases.

DEPS
Depends on the base lifecycle contract bead so this amendment lands after the six-status state machine spec edit.","id":"ddx-5995f3bc","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:beads","area:docs","kind:design","lifecycle-state-machine","migration-gate","phase:2","spec:TD-031"],"owner":"erik","parent":"ddx-349e8e03","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"TD-031","status":"closed","title":"docs: require one-way lifecycle migration and startup gate","updated_at":"2026-05-09T06:03:31.81479045Z","work-heartbeat-at":"2026-05-09T06:00:39.837266099Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:472:{"acceptance":"1. ExecuteBeadReport gains an explicit Disrupted bool (or Disposition variant) set when the loop detects: ctx.Err() != nil, executor exited via SIGKILL/SIGTERM, transport-class provider error, or worker process restart marker.
2. Detection at: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go where ExecutionFailed is set (current lines 513 routing-preflight, 629/637 executor-error, 1011 in execute_bead.go) — each set point evaluates whether the failure is Disrupted.
3. The cooldown branch (lines 1074-1086) checks Disrupted FIRST: if Disrupted, skip SetExecutionCooldown entirely; just Unclaim so the bead returns to ready.
4. shouldSuppressNoProgress unchanged for genuine no-progress (BaseRev == ResultRev AND not Disrupted AND no rationale).
5. New diagnostic event `disruption_detected` with kind, reason, and bead context — emitted when Disrupted is set so operators can see disruption rates.
6. (Optional) Exponential backoff for non-Disrupted no-progress: first failure 5min; second 30min; third 2h; cap at 24h. Reset on success. If implementing: replace flat noProgressCooldown with this curve; document in code comment.
7. Tests:
   - TestLoop_DisruptedExecution_NoCooldown — context cancelled mid-execution; assert no work-retry-after set; bead returns to ready
   - TestLoop_GenuineNoProgress_StillCooldowns — model returns clean with no commit + rationale; assert 6h cooldown set
   - TestLoop_PreflightRejection_NoCooldown — routing preflight fails; assert disruption classification (operator config, not model failure)
   - TestLoop_DisruptionEventEmitted — assert disruption_detected event appears in bead events
8. Manual verification: kill an in-flight worker mid-execution; assert the bead is immediately re-claimable by the next worker (no 6h park).
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.
10. Documentation: update FEAT-006 (or wherever the cooldown policy is documented) to describe Disrupted vs no-progress and the policy rationale.","archived_at":"2026-05-07T04:29:14Z","claimed-at":"2026-05-03T02:17:24Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"163374","closing_commit_sha":"89473d084d436e925934549d31f560e5d6b8702a","created_at":"2026-05-03T01:47:21.609526243Z","description":"The current cooldown logic treats any ExecutionFailed status as evidence the model can't make progress, applying noProgressCooldown=6h when BaseRev == ResultRev (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1074-1086 via shouldSuppressNoProgress at line 1550). This silently freezes important work whenever a worker is disrupted (killed, server restart, network blip, OS issue) — disruption produces ExecutionFailed + no commit, indistinguishable from "model genuinely tried and produced nothing."

OBSERVED (today, 2026-05-02)
After the picker fix investigation found the four "starved" P0 beads (ddx-dc157075, ddx-aee651ec, ddx-29058e2a, ddx-9d55601f) all had work-retry-after timestamps ~3 hours in the future. Investigation per ddx-9d55601f confirmed these were NOT genuine model failures — they were prior worker disruptions during this development session (worker kills, server restarts) being treated as model-gave-up.

User directive: "Cooldown should be reserved for cases where the model is truly unable to make progress on a task."

CURRENT COOLDOWN POLICY (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go)
- LandConflictCooldown = 15min — short cooldown after merge/land conflicts (line 64)
- noProgressCooldown = 6h default — when shouldSuppressNoProgress is true (line 367)
- MaxLoopCooldown = 24h — push_failed, push_conflict, decomposition decline (line 57)

shouldSuppressNoProgress (line 1550): returns true if BaseRev != "" AND ResultRev != "" AND BaseRev == ResultRev. This catches:
- Model wrote nothing AND came back cleanly (true no_progress) ✓
- Worker killed mid-execution before commit (FALSE no_progress — disruption)
- Network/provider error before model started (FALSE no_progress — disruption)
- Routing preflight rejection (FALSE no_progress — config issue)

The mis-classification is the bug.

PROPOSED FIX

Add a Disposition::Disrupted variant (or equivalent flag on ExecuteBeadReport) that explicitly marks results that are NOT evidence of model inability:
- ctx.Err() != nil (cancellation, deadline, parent died)
- syscall.SIGTERM / SIGINT received during execution
- Provider-side 5xx, connection refused, timeout (transport disruption)
- Worker process killed (parent never received clean result)
- Server-restart-detected marker
- Routing preflight rejection (operator config issue, not model failure)

For Disrupted: skip cooldown entirely (or use a tiny defer like 30s) and unclaim the bead so the next worker pickup is immediate.
For genuine no-progress (model returned clean with no commit AND rationale present): keep current 6h cooldown.

ALTERNATIVE: exponential backoff per bead. First failure: 5min; second: 30min; third: 2h; cap at 24h. Resets on any successful attempt or operator clear. This is more forgiving than the current flat 6h on the first hiccup.

PROBABLY BOTH: Disrupted skips cooldown entirely; non-Disrupted failures use exponential backoff starting at 5min, not flat 6h.

DETECTION POINTERS

- ctx.Err() check at the call site that produces ExecutionFailed (line 513, 629, 637, 1011)
- Check for `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/no_changes_rationale.txt` presence — if missing, the model never wrote one (likely disruption)
- Check the executor's exit code: SIGKILL/SIGTERM = disruption; clean exit with empty result = no-progress
- Track parent process restart count or boot ID; if changed since the attempt started, mark Disrupted

NOT IN SCOPE
- Changing the cooldown duration constants themselves
- Reworking the per-bead retry policy framework (separate concern)
- Changing land-conflict or push-failed cooldowns (those are correct as-is)
- The exponential-backoff alternative is OPTIONAL; primary fix is Disrupted classification

INTERSECTION WITH OTHER BEADS
- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): the stay-alive fix prevents some classes of disruption (worker no longer exits on empty poll), but does NOT fix the cooldown-on-disruption mis-classification
- ddx-076147ee (worker client-server architecture TD): if workers become long-lived clients, server-restart disruption goes away by construction. But this fix is needed BEFORE that TD is implemented because the disruption classification is needed regardless.
- ddx-9228a484 C9 (StopCondition enum): may absorb this — the StopCondition enum could include Disrupted as a state. Verify and align.","events_attachment":"ddx-5b3e57f4/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-5b3e57f4","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:2","area:agent","kind:fix","observed-failure"],"owner":"erik","parent":"ddx-e34994e2","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-59cfc8c5","status":"closed","title":"work: distinguish worker-disrupted from model-gave-up; do not cooldown on disruption","updated_at":"2026-05-03T02:30:29.374776912Z","work-heartbeat-at":"2026-05-03T02:29:24.332622724Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:473:{"archived_at":"2026-05-07T04:29:14Z","created_at":"2026-04-15T00:57:36.597150715Z","description":"'ddx agent doctor' currently reports a harness as OK if its binary exists on PATH. This is insufficient: for the embedded 'agent' harness, the actual backend is a remote inference server (LM Studio on bragi/vidar/grendel or a cloud API). A healthy binary + unreachable backend reports OK but cannot do any work.

## Observed failure

During the axon ADR-018 work, 'ddx agent doctor' reported '10/10 harnesses available' while bragi's LM Studio was stopped. Trying to run ddx-agent -provider bragi returned a 502 Bad Gateway. I had to manually curl each host to discover the real state.

## Scope

- Extend 'ddx agent doctor' to probe each provider in the agent config with a small 'models' or 'ping' request within a short timeout
- Report per-provider status alongside per-harness status: provider name, host, last-probe result, last-probe ms, last-probe error
- --quick mode: skip provider probes (current behavior), for fast checks
- Default behavior: probe providers; show a two-column status (harness OK, provider reachable Y/N)
- Integration with work's discovery-driven escalation (ddx-dc231144): the probe result feeds the per-provider cooldown map

## Acceptance

- [ ] doctor probes every provider in the agent config by default
- [ ] Unreachable/5xx providers are reported as degraded, not OK
- [ ] doctor output is machine-readable via --json
- [ ] doctor --quick keeps the old fast-path behavior
- [ ] Integration test: doctor against a fake provider that returns 502 reports the provider as unhealthy","events_attachment":"ddx-5bac3c6b/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-5bac3c6b","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","area:agent","area:exec","kind:bug","gap:review-loop"],"priority":1,"schema_version":1,"status":"closed","title":"exec: harness doctor must probe provider backends, not just binaries","updated_at":"2026-04-15T03:45:01.910986192Z"}

Adding any flag requires editing 5+ places, and forgetting one is silent.\n\nCURRENT STATE (the smell)\n\nEach flag traverses 6 boundaries:\n\n1. cobra extract: cmd.Flags().GetX(\"flag\") → local var (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1573-1611)\n2. positional param: passed to executeLoopWithServer / runExecuteLoopLocal (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1627, ~1941 signature)\n3. loose JSON map: workerSpec map[string]interface{} (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:~1948)\n4. server request struct: hand-maintained in handleStartExecuteLoopWorker (cli/internal/server/server.go:2246-2260)\n5. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal: server.go:2289-2303 → cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49\n6. runWorker per-field consumption: cli/internal/server/workers.go ~656\n\nALSO IN SCOPE — the GraphQL parallel path (per codex review):\n- workerDispatchAdapter at cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:62-75, 123-137 has its own request struct AND its own StartExecuteLoop literal — same parallel-struct smell on a different transport. Unification must DELETE this too, otherwise the refactor fixes REST and leaves GraphQL re-broken.\n\nTwo of those (steps 4 and 5) are typed but maintained in parallel. The 5 dropped flags (opaque_passthrough, max_cost, request_timeout, min_power, max_power) fell out exactly there. PLUS FromRev is also dropped on the server path (cobra flag at agent_cmd.go:1560 + work.go:58, consumed locally at agent_cmd.go:1753 ExecuteBeadRuntime{FromRev: fromRev}, never reaches the server). The bug fix would be local — add fields in 5 places — but the ROOT CAUSE recurs the next time a flag is added.\n\nDESIGN — single canonical ExecuteLoopSpec struct\n\nDefine one struct, used at every layer:\n\n  cobra RunE → parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions)\n                ↓\n       (--local) runExecuteLoopInline(spec)\n                ↓\n       (default) httpSubmitExecuteLoop(spec)  ── JSON ──▶ server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker\n                                                   ↓ (decode INTO THE SAME struct, then Validate + ApplyDefaults)\n                                                startExecuteLoop(spec)\n                                                   ↓ (persist as spec.json — SAME struct, fully-resolved)\n                                                runWorker(spec)\n\nProperties:\n- One struct definition. JSON tags drive both wire format and disk persistence.\n- Cobra binding is the only per-flag code (irreducible — cobra needs to know each flag).\n- --local and server paths consume the same struct; behavior parity is structural, not asserted by tests.\n- Adding a flag = (a) one struct field + (b) one cobra binding. Drop is impossible by construction — the JSON tag round-trips through wire and persistence.\n- DispatchOptions is a SEPARATE small struct holding control-plane flags (--local, --json) that are NOT execution-spec fields and must NOT be persisted on disk.\n\nCRITICAL CONTRACTS (per codex review)\n\n1. Wire format for time.Duration: Go's default time.Duration JSON marshaling produces nanoseconds, but current code sends poll_interval as a string (agent_cmd.go:1969-1971). The unified spec MUST commit to one format — either custom Duration type with string MarshalJSON, or canonical numeric. Pick string (operator-readable spec.json wins) and add UnmarshalJSON tolerating both.\n\n2. Validate + ApplyDefaults lifecycle:\n   - parseExecuteLoopSpec returns spec + DispatchOptions, with cobra defaults applied\n   - Spec.Validate() is called BEFORE HTTP submit (client-side guard)\n   - server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes → calls Spec.ApplyDefaults() → Spec.Validate() BEFORE StartExecuteLoop (server cannot trust the wire)\n   - GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter goes through THE SAME StartExecuteLoop entry point so Validate + ApplyDefaults are guaranteed\n   - spec.json on disk is fully-resolved (no implicit defaults at runWorker time)\n\n3. Forward-compat stance: ddx is single-repo, single-version client/server today. Add SpecVersion int field; server returns 400 with capability list if SpecVersion \u003e supported. Newer-server-older-client: existing fields unchanged, new fields ignored on both sides (the current default). Make this explicit in the AC; do not rely on json's silent unknown-field tolerance as the only contract.\n\n4. Backward-compat for old persisted specs: existing spec.json files in .ddx/workers/ have legacy fields (e.g., min_tier/max_tier in older specs at .ddx/workers/worker-20260422T083934-f77c/spec.json:5-6). UnmarshalJSON must tolerate unknown fields when LOADING from disk (json.Decoder default behavior is fine here; do NOT enable DisallowUnknownFields for disk reads).\n\nLOCATION\n\nLikely cli/internal/agent/spec.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go (a small focused package). Importable from both cli/cmd/ and cli/internal/server/ without import cycles. NOTE: REACH-PROTO (ddx-a946c744) needs to know about this new package as an entry-root for the production-reachability checker.\n\nNOT IN SCOPE\n\n- Other commands (ddx agent run, ddx try, ddx run, MCP agent dispatch, ddx work passthrough variants) — same smell may exist; covered by the sister audit bead ddx-fb290074.\n- Reworking client→server transport (HTTP/JSON stays as-is).\n- Changing validation logic itself — only ensuring it lives on the spec so both paths apply it.\n- Renaming flags or reshaping CLI surface.\n- Reshaping LabelFilter from string to []string — that is a picker semantics change, owned by ddx-9d55601f. Keep LabelFilter as string to keep ddx-9d55601f landing in parallel.\n\nUSER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM (originally what filed this bead)\n- ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server.\n- Root cause: opaque_passthrough was dropped at the server's request struct.
